data.no_dmg_msg_npc: The %1$s tries to inject <npcname>, but fails to penetrate their armor!
data.no_dmg_msg_u: The %1$s tries to inject you, but fails to penetrate your armor!

messages: You dream of warm, alien winds.
You dream of landscapes lit by the light of alien moons.
You stand at the edge of a fissure into strange earth.  Mustard brown gasses caress and lift your fronds
You dream of being vivsected by humans.  One asks if the other thinks you can feel pain.
Trapped in a cage, you mimic the sounds of your guard's spawn.  He threatens you and enters the cage.  He screams, you recall.
It's your birthday.  But everyone has given you these hideous metal and plastic objects.
The stars await you, your chariot of fire is ready.  The holds are full of slaves.
You have transitioned from a dying race to a glorious future.
You have a strange dream about thundering ponderously through ancient, brittle tundras that crackle under your thick round feet.
Your dreams give you a strange, langourous, heavy feeling.

You dream of swinging your heavy head to shake the clinging snow and ice from your large, limpid brown eyes.  The weight is off, as if you had something… extra on either side of your mouth, and though you are surrounded by snow and bitter pelting winds, you feel confident and toasty-warm beneath your shaggy coat.

Your dream is a stream of shaggy loam-brown fur trailing into an ocean of punishing, icy white.  Together, you are all strong.  When you look around, you see elephantine faces looking back from all angles and you know they mirror your own.  You just… know.

You dream of your usual patient languor being interrupted by a flash of white teeth against a carmine-soaked muzzle.  In an instant a thunderous fury overtakes you and you trumpet your rage… right before you bring that trumpeting snout, bring down those heavy spears of ivory on either side of it, down upon your attacker.  They lie, bones shattered, bleeding out their red into the icy white and causing it to steam  Just like that, your calm is restored.

You dream of slowly, patiently, plodding through the world to go from goal to goal, unrushed and unworried, for you are far too large and tough to kill for anyone or anything to bother trying to attack.  And if they do… it'll be the last mistake of their life.  Waking up gives you a brief jolt of fear and dysphoria, for your body feels so weak and fragile and incorrect compared to the powerful thing you know you are.

Your thoughts within your dream may be slow, and it may take you some time to reach a conclusion, but what's the rush?  What's the hurry?  You are a huge and ancient thing with a pedigree that stretches back to a time before sapient life first had the gall to raise a sharpened stick and call itself superior.  You are huge and powerful and all that work against you shall fall.  You… you have all the time in the world, now.
Life is lonely without a family of Tusked Ones beside you, thundering as one through this desolate world in search of the hidden places.  Perhaps… perhaps you should start your family.

data.description: NPC trait that makes monsters see it as a Prepnet.  It is a bug if you have it.

data.//: Should be out for two minutes.  Nightmare realm becomes home
data.iv_message: You look into your own cells and change them.
data.iv_sleep_message: You gaze inside, past your fear, past good and evil, into the future.
data.memorial_message: Prepared the way into the galaxy.
data.mutagen_message: Strange memories of another world overwhelm you…
data.name: YUGGOTH

data.iv_message: You heart races, and you glance around for poachers.
data.mutagen_message: You feel a desire to trumpet and toot.
data.name: Mastodon

data.description: Your species was created as the ultimate shock troop.  Inherit the earth, pedigree your ancestors, crush the foe.
Fleshy fronds grown from your scalp function like organic heat sinks.  They allow you to live comfortably within the mi-go atmosphere.
data.name: Mi-go acclimatization

data.description: A set of tentacles surrounds your mouth.  They allow you to eat twice as fast.  Slightly decreases wet penalties.
data.name: Facial Tentacles

data.description: Skin tabs and odd flaps of skin surround your mouth.  They don't affect your eating, but are unpleasant to look at.
data.name: Mouth Tendrils

data.description: Your body becomes much more efficient at distributing heat from itself.
data.name: Warm Natured

data.description: You see the world a little differently today.  Intelligence + 1
data.name: Neural Improvement

data.description: You are beginning to see a way off this planet.  Intelligence + 2
data.name: Expanded Consciousness

data.description: As humans uplifted our pets in the final years this process is lifting you to a new level of intelligence.  Intelligence + 4
data.name: Extremely Smart

data.description: You can see the connections from A to B to C to D.  You look at fellow survivors like you are imagining taking them apart.  Intelligence + 5
data.name: Alien Intelligence

data.description: Today is the day to start juggling.  Dexterity + 1
data.name: Enhanced Reactions

data.description: You are starting to move like they do.  Dexterity + 2
data.name: Quickening

data.description: You no longer move like a human, others find it disconcerting.  Dexterity + 4
data.name: Otherworldly Grace

data.description: You see some new muscles that you aren't sure you've seen on humans before.  Strength + 1
data.name: New Muscles

data.description: Fibrous tissue seems to be spreading through your body.  Strength + 2
data.name: Muscle Fiber

data.description: Your ears have split into a series of tuberous projections.  They waggle towards far away sounds.
data.name: Mi-go Ears

data.description: When were you ever like these creatures, helpless before the changes ravaging this world.
data.name: Detachment

data.description: You have a sinister aspect to your demeanor, no longer part of this world.  You feel no sorrow at the deaths of humans and your brain now processes combat skills more efficiently as you mentally dissect your opponents.
data.name: Eldritch

data.description: This death throes of this world are the birthing pangs of yours.
data.name: YUGGOTH

data.description: Your sweat now smells otherworldly, and not in a good way.  The mi-go still hate you but at least they aren't offended by your smell.
data.name: Fetid scent

data.description: You are offputting to others.  You're mannerisms have changed as if human interaction is becoming foreign.
data.name: Eerie

data.description: You have a pair of large, veiny wings.  They don't appear to be made for this atmosphere but they grant a powerful buffeting attack, and reduce falling damage.
data.name: Mi-go Wings

data.description: You can now breathe the gasses the mi-go thrive in.
data.name: Mi-go breathe

data.description: You come from uplifted animal stock.  This decreases morale penalties for being wet.
data.name: Uplifted

data.description: You are capable of carrying far more than someone with similar strength could.  Your maximum weight carried is increased by 60%.
data.name: Hauler

data.description: Your skin has patches of light fur.  This has no impact on your life except marking you as not fully human.
data.name: Patched Fur

attacks.attack_text_u: You kick %s with your massive feet

data.description: Your feet have grown massive and ready to support huge weight.  This allows kicking attacks to do much more damage, provides 2 armor, and removes the need to wear shoes; however, you cannot wear normal size shoes.  Reduces wet effects.
data.name: Crushing Feet

data.description: You have a pair of bumps on your face above your mouth and beside your nose.
data.name: Face Bumps

data.description: You have a pair of small tusks on your face.  They allow you to make a weak piercing goring attack.
data.name: Tusks

attacks.attack_text_u: You stab %s with your pointed tusks

data.description: You have a pair of long, pointed tusks, like someone weaponized an elephant.  They allow you to make a strong piercing headbutt attack, but prevent wearing mouthgear that is not made of fabric.
data.name: Pointed Tusks

data.description: Your face resembles that of an elephant, with a significant trunk.  It looks fierce but prevents wearing mouthgear.  Prehensile and able to perform some fine detail work.

Your hands have fused into three fingered paws.  Fine manipulation is a challenge: permanent hand encumbrance of 10, difficulty with delicate craftwork, and your gloves don't fit.  But they handle water better.
data.name: Three Fingered

data.description: You have a trumpeting elephantine voice.  threatening NPCs will be easier, but lying will very hard.
data.name: Trumpeting Voice

data.description: Genetic tampering made your body incredibly strong.  Strength + 7.
An experimental cryosleep procedure has permanently altered your body, allowing you to better tolerate cold temperatures.
Time spent in high altitudes and low temperatures have adapted you to them, and you're slightly better at resisting the cold.
Genetic defects have made your body incredibly strong.  Strength + 7.
The bottoms of your feet are tough like an animals.  You receive no movement penalty for not wearing shoes.
data.name: Leathered Feet

data.description: Your skin is tough.  Cutting damage is slightly reduced for you.  Slightly decreases wet penalties.
data.name: Skinfolds

data.description: A common flaw in your genemod, brought on by increased muscle mass and bone density.  Moving over rough terrain will slow you down more than normal.
You have problems with eating meat.  It's possible for you to eat it, but you will suffer morale penalties and obtain less nutrition from it.
data.name: Herbivorous Ancestry

data.description: Your scent is particularly strong.  It's vaguely offensive to humans, and animals that track your scent will do so more easily.
data.name: Strong Scent

data.description: You make more noise while walking.  You're also more likely to set off traps.
You can't move as fast as most, resulting in a 10% speed penalty on flat ground.
data.name: Slow and Steady

data.description: Short fur has grown to cover your entire body, providing slight protection from cold.
Thick brown fur has grown to cover your entire body, providing a slight protection against attacks, and some protection from cold.
Your fur has grown out and thickened, providing noticeable protection from attacks as well as considerable insulation.
You can survive injuries that would incapacitate humans: you get a 20% bonus to all hit points.  Stacks with Tough, etc.
data.name: Immovable

data.description: Not much scares you.  You get a 30% bonus to all hit points.  Stacks with Tough, etc.
data.name: Unstoppable

data.description: You can simply take the punishment from lesser beings and keep going.  You get a 40% bonus to all hit points.  Stacks with Tough, etc.
data.name: Meatshield

data.description: You have grown noticeably taller and broader.  Much of it is useful muscle mass (Strength +2), but you find it throws off your balance and you get in your own way (+10 torso and arm encumbrance).
data.name: Elephantine

data.description: You carry your rugged body with the dignity you deserve.  Strength +2.
data.name: Large

data.description: You have grown even more massive, to the point where you cannot usefully fit into human-sized clothing or vehicles.  Much of it is powerful muscle mass (Strength +4), but it's a real pain to haul around.
data.name: Freakishly Gigantic

data.description: Your cardiovascular system has caught up with your muscular physique, so who needs pathetic human cars?  Strength +4.
data.name: Gigantic

data.description: You smell like exactly like a shaggy elephant would, assuming it sweated, which you do.  Monsters that track scent will find you very easily, and humans will react poorly.
data.name: Terrible Odor

data.description: You require more food than most people, but recover stamina slightly faster.
data.name: Constant Eater

data.description: You require more food than others but your ability to go the distance in both combat and athleticism is expanded.
data.name: Large Diet

data.description: Your body needs more water than usual to flush the denser waste products.
data.name: High Thirst

data.description: Ugh, out of water already?  You need about twice the fluids of an average human.
data.name: Giant Thirst

data.description: Your weight must be delicately placed lest the ground give way.  You move 10% slower.
data.name: Ponderous

data.description: Your muscles are quite slow to move.  You move 25% slower.
data.name: Very Deliberate

data.dynamic_line: What level of hell is this?  I spent weeks in a sulforous hell being tortured.
Then they started cutting me open and taking out my organs but adding new ones.
Now that I finally am able to move and have some freedom, I plan on finding some of these demons and punching through them until I find the devil himself.
I heard the song before the aliens captured me.  I did what I could to help the other Bonesingers.
One day I was being chased by some aliens and suddenly I was ambushed by another one that shot me with some kind of pain ray.
Eventually they took the bones from my body.  It's strange though, I still hear the song calling to me, even in this new body.
I just spent what felt like weeks in a dream that I was acting as a targeting computer for some kind of alien turret.
Now I'm in some kind of Mary Shelley nightmare?  I mean at least I have mobility.

responses.text: What does that have to do with anything?

data.dynamic_line: I'm going to keep going with this dream, hopefully soon I'll get to one where I can fly.
Antes do Cataclisma eu era o cientista que encabeçava um laboratório militar no meu país.  Foram dias difíceis…

Nós estávamos pesquisando um remédio que curasse um virus altamente letal na época, mas os resultados dos testes mostravam que o remédio era mais letal do que o próprio virus.  Então, ao invés de recolherem os remédios das prateleiras, eles nos forçaram a vendê-lo como sendo a cura para o vírus.
Bem… aqueles sem cérebro seguiram cegamente o líder.  Quanto àqueles com algum cérebro… bom, acho que você está conversando com um deles agora
Things have been getting weird for the last couple of years.  Then we started hearing rumors of strange lights in the sky at night.
One night I heard my neighbor calling for me to come help him.  I went outside wondering what he could possibly need my help with in the middle of the night.

Some kind of hideos alien was standing over my neighbors disembowled corpse.  It looked at me and said 'You're a great neighbor' then everything faded to black.  I feel like everything in my body fits differently now.

I worked in a top secret lab.  Things kept getting crazier and crazier outside and the scientists inside started making desperate experiments.  They worked on something called interdimensional portals.
The eggheads kept sayig we needed to find a way out of here before everything went sideways.  So we needed to open more portals even if we hadn't had time to take all the precautions.

One day they opened a portal trying to find an earthlike planet and suddenly portals were opening all over the lab and these fast and dangerous aliens overwhelmed the lab.  They killed most of the staff and took the rest prisoner.
Monsters!  Monsters everywhere.  Everyone is suspect, how do I know you aren't a monster?
You can't trust anyone these days.  Everyone is either a cannibal or a monster or a government spook.

responses.text: Why would you trust me then?

data.dynamic_line: Because, what if I'm the monster?
If you think this Universe is bad, you should see some of the others.
Believing something existed and then finding out it didn't was like reaching the top of the stairs and thinking there was one more step.

responses.text: What does that have to do with anything?

data.dynamic_line: I think a lot of psychopaths are just geniuses who drove so fast that they lost control.

data.dynamic_line: I underwent experimental treatments paid for by the cartels to become a more fearsome enforcer.
Half of the guys I underwent gene therapy with died, but hey if I had to guess 90 percent of everyone is dead now?
Only the insane will prosper.
As a corporate wetworks specialist I'm used to crazy situations.  But this is like nothing I've ever experienced.

responses.text: What are you going to do now?

data.dynamic_line: I'm gonna find somewhere that people are making a stand.  And if I can agree with their plans I'll make my stand with them.
Right now you are looking like the best leader I've met so far in this Cataclysm.
I was born at the bottom of the habitat.  No silver spoon in my mouth.
I saw my best friend die in front of me by the time I was eight.
I guess it prepared me for everyone dying yesterday.
I've spent my life in the hive.  When things started turning strange I snuck out of the city.

responses.text: You seem like you are out of your element.

data.dynamic_line: I've never seen so many trees outside of a video.  And there is so much wildlife.
I'm going to live my best life in a dead world.
There's so much sky out here.  It's horrible.

responses.text: You seem like you are out of your element.

data.dynamic_line: It might be worse than the dead rising.
I'm going to kill my way back into the city so I can get out from under this sky.

data.job_description: I'm looking for lizard mutagen… this world is no place for humans anymore, and I don't plan to keep being one.
data.name: Lizard Mutant

data.dynamic_line: When the government gave up on saving the depths of the city someone decided to use tactical nukes and buy time for the retreat.
It blew an opening in the horde roaming the city and allowed me to escape.
Every choice has consequences though.  But every consequence is probably worth it, if it's the only way to survive.
I was working in a reactor when something big started smashing everything.
My coworkers and I spiked the reactor to prevent the deaths of hundreds of thousands.
I guess the other workers are probably all dead anyway now.
Things were good.  I had a good job working for a zaibatsu.  I was respected, and had people working for me.  I mostly ignored the changes that were happening in the city, they were beneath me.

Slowly fewer and fewer of the cubicle drones came into the office.  Security notices piled up about routes through the city to avoid.  Finally, one day I came in and you could hear the riots coming closer and some kind of animalistic roars, gigantic animals.

That last day security and scientists came up from the underground lab and rounded up all the remaining staff.  One of them said that this was the only chance any of us had of surviving.  They started injecting people at gunpoint.
I was a biohacker by trade before the Cataclysm.  I made a living out of bootlegging medicines, drugs and other copywritten biological products.
Imagine my surprise when I found myself infected with some kind of alien stem cells.
It's certainly helped me survive so far.  Was there even a choice not to take advantage of that?

dynamic_line.no: I… can move again.  I have a real body!  Is this a body?

responses.text: So you must have seen some weird stuff?
I built this body and rescued you…
I can put you back in the pieces I found you in.
No one deserves the hell you've been through.  I hope you find peace.

data.dynamic_line: *crackle* So why would you do this?

responses.text: Come with me.  We can help each other out.
We both go our separate ways.  Enjoy your freedom.

data.dynamic_line: …This is just another torture isn't it!

responses.text: I went to a lot of trouble to provide you with a body.
For all you know, I did.  I'm being nice for now.  You'd better hope that it lasts.

data.dynamic_line: Okay, okay, *sizzle* I'm sorry!  Don't hurt me again!  Anything to stay away from those fiends!

responses.text: Follow me and do my bidding, then.
No, *I'm* sorry, I didn't mean that.  Go do what you want.

data.dynamic_line: …You're one of those mocking monsters!  I'm going to tear you apart!

responses.text: Not if I kill you first!
I'm sorry!  I shouldn't have said that!

data.dynamic_line: I don't know what's happening but I'm going back home.
I was created to be a billionaire's companion.
Their wealth and connections didn't save them.
But they built me with the ability to survive a world beyond imagining.
A resort created a whole line of Uplifts just like me.
We should have created a whole village of just us away from all the humans once the Cataclysm came.
Maybe we did, haha.
I grew up on an island with a doctor.
I had so many brothers and sisters.
We ate him.
The zoo designed me to be a link between my original species and humans studying us.
I was there when the Cataclysm happened.  Many animals were infected.
Zombears are terrifying creatures of destruction.
I was part of a black ops paramilitary unit.  I still don't know if I worked for the government or a corporation.
My team was sent in to a lab on a purge in fire mission.  The things I saw in there haunt me.
Others may have survived but I doubt it.  I certainly hope they haven't come back.
I'm a vatgrown, an almost man, a MacDuff.  I was a product in the Old World.
Now there are too few people left to differentiate.  Other than those morons afraid of mutants.
Now I choose the cause I'll die for.
Staying at the family compounds scattered across New England, we've managed to maintain some organization post Cataclysm.
The Whately family has been outcast in these parts for centuries.  But it made us stronger as a family.
We've been talking to those from other places from the beginning.
All the earth was flame in a holocaust of ecstasy and freedom.

data.job_description: When the end came, we were ready.  Now I grow food for my phyle.
I've been studying the mi-go for years…
data.name: mi-go enthusiast

data.job_description: Our family has been through hard times before.  We know how to survive.
data.name: Whately Cousin

data.job_description: Friend to outsiders…
data.name: Aspiring Frankenstein

data.//: This group picks out background traits that could apply to specific Aftershock cyborg survivor.
This group picks out background traits that could apply to specific Aftershock cyborg survivor.

data.job_description: I've survived all my life with the whole world against me, how is this any different.
I made a good living working in the shadows of the old world.  I can survive it's twilight.

dynamic_line.no: You look like one of my niece's lab assistants.
yes: Are you ready to leave this marble?

responses.text: Can I do anything for you?  Are you as crazy as your niece?
I'm going on my way now.

data.dynamic_line: Are you here because you are morally flexible and willing to take questionable directions from obviously insane people.

responses.text: Your niece is looking for you.
How did you survive the Cataclysm?
Quite the setup you have here.

data.dynamic_line: This is a Whately family property.  And you are an intruder.

responses.text: I think there's been a mistake.

data.dynamic_line: She's always been so precocious!  I knew that she was going places and we'd see great things from her.

responses.text: She needed a lab assistant, do you?

data.dynamic_line: The Whately family has been part of the fabric of New England since before the Revolutionary War.  And I have been growing our interests for the last few decades.
Muttering 'They whispered of a prison.  Ragnarok engine humming.  Wrapped they in darkness dreaming'.  He shakes himself, 'Anyway it looks like something woke up, for at least a little bit.'
This is a time of great danger and great opportunity and it must not be squandered.

data.description: Find self healing polymers.

dialogue.accepted: Don't forget to tell me when you have them.
dialogue.advice: Hospitals, DIY geneticists.
dialogue.describe: I need 4 self healing polymers from wherever you can find them.
dialogue.failure: Put a stopper in death?  No you never will.
dialogue.offer: My experiments are at a critical point.  Could you find about… 4 self healing polymers for me?
dialogue.rejected: Then why are you even here?  Just let me know if you reconsider.
dialogue.success: My niece shared her research, show me if it works.
dialogue.success_lie: Thanks for trying…  I guess.

data.name: In the absence of life

data.description: I need a neurosynaptic interface matrix.

dialogue.accepted: Without this all that's possible is 'Ye liveliest awfulness'.
dialogue.advice: You're going to have to hunt this down.
dialogue.describe: I need one neurosynaptic interface matrix.
dialogue.failure: Do you have any head cybernetics?  I could possibly salvage one out of your skull.
dialogue.offer: I want to test something and I need a viable version to reverse engineer.  Bring me one neurosynaptic interface matrix.
dialogue.rejected: You'd give up a new world just to bring back Foodplace!
dialogue.success: Have this.
dialogue.success_lie: How could you possibly lie to me about an object you have not put in my hands.

data.name: Making Connections

data.description: Bring me 3 alpha mutagen.

dialogue.accepted: Better living through chemistry.
dialogue.advice: If you can't make it yourself go hit up a lab.  Be prepared for anything in there.
dialogue.describe: I need three alpha mutagen.
dialogue.failure: You are keeping me from my experiments.  Would you like to become one?
dialogue.inquire: Did you get taller since the last time I saw you?
dialogue.offer: I know that one of the corporations were creating a so called Alpha mutagen.  I need three doses to prepare the body.
dialogue.rejected: Would you take it yourself?
dialogue.success: I've got something more potent for you this time.
dialogue.success_lie: Lies and the lying liars who tell them.

data.name: Alpha Mutagen

data.description: We need a strong base for our experiment.

dialogue.accepted: Are you ready for a little necromancy?
dialogue.advice: Look for surgical centers inside the labs.
dialogue.describe: I need an unconscious broken cyborg.  They are already brain dead so it won't be murder.
dialogue.failure: What kinds of body modifications do you have?
dialogue.inquire: So where are my fresh parts?
dialogue.offer: If you go to labs you should find some broken cyborg experiments.  Bring an inactive one to me.
dialogue.rejected: I'm telling you they're already brain dead.
dialogue.success: I've got something more potent for you this time.
dialogue.success_lie: Lies and the lying liars who tell them.

data.name: Broken Cyborg

data.//: Sometime consider changing the end effect of this item and making the test site it's own quest.
data.description: Find 6 living brains in jars.

dialogue.accepted: I knew I could count on your generosity.
dialogue.advice: Brain blasters, that's what I call them.  Some kind of automated weapon system the mi-go use.
dialogue.describe: I need some brains, but brains that are still living.  We've got our own brain blasters, but I won't risk them in experiments.
dialogue.failure: I can't be Dr Frankenstein unless you get me these.
dialogue.inquire: They speak sometimes.  I wonder if they still think.
dialogue.offer: If this works I may be able to give some of these poor souls bodies again.  Six should do.
dialogue.rejected: I would have given you the title of Whately family Igor.
dialogue.success: Go somewhere, far away, and then connect the power system.  Come back and tell me how things went.
dialogue.success_lie: Thanks for trying…  I guess.

data.name: A-Mi-go, You-go find me some brains

data.description: A group of bionic preppers who had expected the collapse of the economy and global chaos, instead they were slightly more ready than others for the Cataclysm.
data.name: PrepNet Phyle

data.description: The Whately's are an old New England family of eccentrics.  Eccentrics being the kind words used about them.
data.name: Whately Family

data.//: Make a Frankenstein BG story
data.job_description: The mi-go took me and vivisected me.  Now you've given me a body again.
data.name: Abomination

data.//: An abomination you have revived.

dynamic_line.no: New test subjects!  I'm so glad you showed up!

responses.text: Can I do anything for you?  Do I want to?
I'm going on my way now.

data.dynamic_line: Millyficent Whately.  I'm so glad you finally arrived.  It's been a while since I last received new lab partners.

responses.text: I'm so hungry, can you help me?
You are aware of the Cataclysm?
I am your new lab partner.

data.dynamic_line: Welcome to my lab!  Here we explore the boundaries of the possible beyond the reach of small minds.

responses.text: Can you give me something to eat then?
I think there's been a mistake.

data.dynamic_line: No, no, no!  No food without succesfully completed experiments.  We mustn't break the contract!

responses.text: What do I have to do?
I don't think you are well.

data.dynamic_line: Well after I did my graduate thesis on theoretical xenobiology the government hired me to join the most interesting program.  Yes there were deaths but we learned so much.
What I know is that I pay well for test subjects and I deliver results and at this rate we will not be releasing on time!
Ever since they moved me to my own lab my research has progressed so much faster.

data.description: Find fetid goop.

dialogue.accepted: Don't forget to tell me when you have them.
dialogue.advice: Find a mi-go base, find a way inside, smash things until you find what I need.
dialogue.describe: I need 14 fetid goops from mi-go locations.
dialogue.failure: You are holding back the evolution of the human race.
dialogue.offer: My experiments are at a critical point.  Could you find about… 14 fetid goops for me?
dialogue.rejected: Then why are you even here?  Just let me know if you reconsider.
dialogue.success: Taste this.
dialogue.success_lie: Thanks for trying…  I guess.

data.name: Migo biology

data.description: Find 56 chunks of mi-go resin.

dialogue.accepted: My notes suggest that their building materials are alive.  I need you to help me confirm that.
dialogue.advice: Find their buildings, jackhammer them.  What did you do before the world changed for the better, cold call salesman?
dialogue.describe: I need 50 chunks of mi-go resin.
dialogue.failure: I wonder if I could trade you to them for what I need?
dialogue.offer: I need to understand more about their environment and how they create it.  This research wasn't even possible previously.  Bring me fifty chunks of mi-go resin.
dialogue.rejected: You can't just walk away from this.  We're changing the world!
dialogue.success: Drink this.
dialogue.success_lie: Did you act on Broadway?  Because I bet that skill isn't useful anymore.

data.name: Migo Resin

data.description: Bring me 3 mutagen.

dialogue.accepted: Mutagen makes the world go round, it's definitely more valuable than love.
dialogue.advice: If you can't make it yourself go hit up a government lab.  Be prepared for anything in there.
dialogue.describe: I need three mutagen.
dialogue.failure: You are keeping me from my experiments.  Would you like to become one?
dialogue.inquire: Mutagen?  Why are you in front of me if not?
dialogue.offer: I need some mutagen and I don't want to spend the time making it myself.  Bring me three doses, if you would be so kind.
dialogue.rejected: Are you scared of where I'm taking you?
dialogue.success: I've got something more potent for you this time.
dialogue.success_lie: Lies and the lying liars who tell them.

data.name: Mutagen

data.description: Find 2 humming hearts.

dialogue.accepted: I have some relatives you should meet, if you don't mind hard work for the advancement of science.
dialogue.advice: Brain blasters, that's what I call them.  Some kind of automated weapon system the mi-go use.
dialogue.describe: Parts, parts, parts.  Bring me some hearts.
dialogue.failure: I can't be Dr Frankenstein unless you get me these.
dialogue.inquire: They speak sometimes.  I wonder if they still think.
dialogue.offer: The Whately's are known in these parts for gathering body parts.  Ha!  That's just rumors and hearsay.  But I do need two humming hearts.
dialogue.rejected: Even I understand this is dangerous.
dialogue.success: Let's see what improvements we can divine from these beauties.
dialogue.success_lie: Thanks for trying…  I guess.

data.name: Humming Hearts

data.description: Cyrus and his family were poorly liked even for Whately's before the end of the world.  They went so far off grid that they may not even know anything has changed.  I need to know he's alive and where his research has taken him.

dialogue.accepted: Of course you said yes, you want to know what he's found just as much as I do!
dialogue.advice: I've given you directions to that branch of the family's bunker.  Yes I said bunker did you think he'd be in a tower?
dialogue.describe: My great uncle Cyrus had an unconventional relationship with both his neighbors and the mi-go before the Cataclysm.  I'd like to know where he has gotten with his research since I haven't heard from him in years.
dialogue.failure: I hope he didn't meet Leandrus' fate…
dialogue.offer: Can you go find my uncle and let him know where I am?
dialogue.rejected: Oh well.  I was hoping to teach you some new things, so you could start your own experiments.
dialogue.success: Thanks, now that he knows I'm here he's sent some of my cousins.  Now that I have more disposable help, take a copy of my early research.
dialogue.success_lie: What good does this do us?

data.name: Find my great uncle Cyrus Whately

data.job_description: Humans created me.  Let's see what I can be on my own.
data.name: Mastodon Uplift

data.//: PrepNet Phyle Gardener.
Abomination.  Change the chat if this loads.

dynamic_line.no: Howdy!  You seem new, what brings you here?

responses.text: Can I do anything for you?
How do I join the phyle?
I'm going on my way now.

data.dynamic_line: I'm a gardener for PrepNet Phyle.  Who are you?

responses.text: I am a beggar who really needs something to eat.
I am just a fellow survivor.
I'm bringing order back to the land.

data.dynamic_line: This is a PrepNet Phyle orchard.  We were a community of survivalists gathering resources to prepare for climate change, but it left us better prepared for what actually happened than most.

responses.text: Can you give me something to eat then?
Only the insane will prosper, I guess.

data.dynamic_line: This food has to go back to our core communities.  But maybe we can trade some food for services
You have to earn the right to trade with us.  Plus we created our own currency before the Cataclysm
This is just an outpost, you'd have to find our main camp and currently we don't want you to.

responses.text: I didn't want to join your club anyway.

data.dynamic_line: Here?  Fruits and berries.  Maybe the occasional piece of farm equipment, but you need crypto coins

responses.text: Crypto coins?  Are you insane, there isn't any global internet now.

data.dynamic_line: Around <interval>.

I got introduced to the movement through Mutual Aid Society.  From there it became an all consuming part of my life to prepare for a post consumption world.  It seems to have worked better for us than for many others.
It can get lonely out here between caravans.  But there's plenty of work and my Cyber Mastiffs.  If a horde comes through we hunker down in a nearby shelter if it's too large for us.

We use so-called crypto coins for money here.  They were a pre-Cataclysm attempt to abstract money even further and create electronic cash.  Now it's a joke because we use physical coins.  Barter only gets you so far.  And one day we'll have the Net back up.

data.description: Find 50 fur pelts.

dialogue.accepted: Don't forget to tell me when you have them.
dialogue.advice: There's a lot of places to look.  The entire outdoors is full of wild animals.
dialogue.describe: I need 50 fur pelts.
dialogue.failure: Don't worry about it, it's not that important.
dialogue.inquire: How is the search going?  Have you found'em?
dialogue.offer: I've got to maintain the orchard but at nights I work on prepping winter clothes.  Could you find about… 50 fur pelts for me?
dialogue.rejected: It's okay, I can do without them.  Just let me know if you reconsider.
dialogue.success: I really apreciate your help.  Don't worry, you won't leave empty-handed.
dialogue.success_lie: Thanks for trying…  I guess.

data.name: Big Game Hunting

data.description: Find 50 flour.

dialogue.accepted: Thanks for accepting this task.  Otherwise I might kill a stranger for a sandwich.  Just kidding.
dialogue.advice: Flour is not that hard to find.  I'd look for them in residental areas and professional kitchens or bakeries.
dialogue.describe: I need 50 flour.
dialogue.failure: It's not a big deal, it isn't that urgent.
dialogue.offer: Flour would let me break the monotony of fruit and meats.
dialogue.rejected: Just let me know if you change your mind.
dialogue.success: Thank you for your help.  Here's your reward.
dialogue.success_lie: Thanks for trying…  I guess.

data.name: Bread

data.description: Find 5 gallon jugs.

dialogue.accepted: Thank you for accepting.  I'm almost out after the last caravan.
dialogue.advice: Gallon jugs are not that rare to find.  They are commonly found in kitchens.
dialogue.describe: I need storage for various liquid products when the fruits come in.
dialogue.failure: Don't worry about it, it's not that important.
dialogue.inquire: Do you have those jugs now?
dialogue.offer: Every season we produce so much fruit.  Some of it will be turned into juice and some into alcohol.  I need easy containers to load it on the caravan.
dialogue.rejected: I guess I'll have to make stomach waterskins.
dialogue.success: I am grateful for the help you've done.  I have one more task to do.
dialogue.success_lie: Thanks for trying…  I guess.

data.name: Gallon Jugs

data.description: Find 5 burnt out CBMs.

dialogue.accepted: Thank you for accepting.  You're really making yourself useful to the phyle.
dialogue.advice: Zombie scientists, technicians, shocker zombies.  These are not undertaken lightly.
dialogue.describe: We have the ability to manufacture CBMs but lack resources.
dialogue.failure: Don't worry about it, it's not that important.
dialogue.inquire: Do you have those CBMs now?
dialogue.offer: If you can bring me burnt out CBMs we can recycle them into new ones.
dialogue.rejected: These materials are often in dangerous areas.  I understand your decision.
dialogue.success: I am grateful for the help you've done.  I think we might be able to introduce you to others soon.
dialogue.success_lie: Thanks for trying…  I guess.

data.name: Burnt Out CBMs

dialogue.accepted: Don't forget to tell me when you have them.
dialogue.advice: Find a mi-go base, find a way inside, smash things until you find what I need.
dialogue.describe: I need 14 fetid goops from mi-go locations.
dialogue.failure: You are holding back the evolution of the human race.
dialogue.offer: My experiments are at a critical point.  Could you find about… 14 fetid goops for me?
dialogue.rejected: Then why are you even here?  Just let me know if you reconsider.
dialogue.success: Taste this.
dialogue.success_lie: Thanks for trying…  I guess.

data.name: Migo biology

data.description: Find 56 chunks of mi-go resin.

dialogue.accepted: My notes suggest that their building materials are alive.  I need you to help me confirm that.
dialogue.advice: Find their buildings, jackhammer them.  What did you do before the world changed for the better, cold call salesman?
dialogue.describe: I need 56 chunks of mi-go resin.
dialogue.failure: I wonder if I could trade you to them for what I need?
dialogue.offer: I need to understand more about their environment and how they create it.  This research wasn't even possible previously.  Bring me fifty six chunks of mi-go resin.
dialogue.rejected: You can't just walk away from this.  We're changing the world!
dialogue.success: Drink this.
dialogue.success_lie: Did you act on Broadway?  Because I bet that skill isn't useful anymore.

data.name: Migo Resin

data.description: Bring me 3 mutagen.

dialogue.accepted: Mutagen makes the world go round, it's definitely more valuable than love.
dialogue.advice: If you can't make it yourself go hit up a government lab.  Be prepared for anything in there.
dialogue.describe: I need three mutagen.
dialogue.failure: You are keeping me from my experiments.  Would you like to become one?
dialogue.inquire: Mutagen?  Why are you in front of me if not?
dialogue.offer: I need some mutagen and I don't want to spend the time making it myself.  Bring me three doses, if you would be so kind.
dialogue.rejected: Are you scared of where I'm taking you?
dialogue.success: I've got something more potent for you this time.
dialogue.success_lie: Lies and the lying liars who tell them.

data.name: Mutagen

data.description: Find 2 humming hearts.

dialogue.accepted: I have some relatives you should meet, if you don't mind hard work for the advancement of science.
dialogue.advice: Brain blasters, that's what I call them.  Some kind of automated weapon system the mi-go use.
dialogue.describe: Parts, parts, parts.  Bring me some hearts.
dialogue.failure: I can't be Dr Frankenstein unless you get me these.
dialogue.inquire: They speak sometimes.  I wonder if they still think.
dialogue.offer: The Whately's are known in these parts for gathering body parts.  Ha!  That's just rumors and hearsay.  But I do need two humming hearts.
dialogue.rejected: Even I understand this is dangerous.
dialogue.success: Let's see what improvements we can divine from these beauties.
dialogue.success_lie: Thanks for trying…  I guess.

data.name: Humming Hearts

dynamic_line.no: We don't get outsiders around here all that much.

responses.text: Can I do anything for you?
How do I join the family?
I'm going on my way now.

data.dynamic_line: I'm a member of the Whately Family.  Who are you?

responses.text: I am lost and really need something to eat.
I am just a fellow survivor.
I've done some tasks for your relatives.

data.dynamic_line: Welcome to Whately lands.  Behave yourself or we'll harvest you for parts.

responses.text: Can you give me something to eat then?

data.dynamic_line: We aren't in the habit of breaking bread with outsiders.

responses.text: How do I join your family?

data.dynamic_line: Then let's see what you've got to trade.

responses.text: When will you have more in stock?

data.dynamic_line: Around <interval>.
Only blood allowed, and the family Elders can make you of the blood.
I was born part of the family.  We've never been accepted here but our wealth and talents have kept us alive through the centuries.
Family keeps me busy and our experiments and projects take up the rest of my time.

data.description: Installed on your body is a set of small retractable wind turbines.  When activated, they will deploy and slowly harvest wind power to recharge your power level.

You've worked for some nasty people.  People who installed a bomb at the top of your spine.  They are all dead now but there is unfortunately a dead man switch if you don't check in roughly every thirty days.  You need this out and fast.
Concealed in your head is a single shot .40 pistol.  Activate the bionic to fire and reload the skullgun.

Your hands have been outfitted with precise soldering tools, wire cutters, and cable spools.  They're too small to use in most crafting, but in the absence of proper machinery, they're essential for creating bionics without better tools.

From your spot at the top of the tower you were working on, you had a good view of the end of the world; of streaks of light falling from the sky, blossoming into mushroom clouds, barely a flicker on the horizon before your optical dampers flicked down.  You rushed to safety before the shockwaves could blast you from your perch.  The bionic implants included with your contract might come in handy now…
data.name: Radio Tower Technician

data.description: A sudden power failure woke you from the cryosleep sarcophagus in which you had entombed yourself lifetimes ago.  It seems the emergency reanimation procedure has damaged your memory, and now, bearing almost no knowledge of your past life, you must find a way to survive in a ruined and alien world.
data.name: Cryosleep Survivor

data.description: You were hired to market Rivtech's products through TV and were on your way to the studio when the bombs hit.  You enter now the end of the world with nothing but the snazzy clothes on your back and a bunch of plutonium-powered toys.

When the Cataclysm came, you were getting into your gear, prepared for the first manned entrance through a stabilized portal in the lab - just a quick five-minute scouting trip.  You were the last one left in the room when the portal snapped shut and all hell broke loose.  Now that you know what was on the other end, maybe that was a mercy.

Grown in some corporate laboratory to provide the perfect bodyguard, with nothing to live for but your client.  The Cataclysm ignored social strata and somehow you outlived your master.  Now no one knows the conditioning words to force your obedience.  While the world burns, you get your first taste of life.
data.name: Vatgrown Bodyguard

data.description: You worked as a technician in a sterile, high-security facility concerned with the fabrication of bionic implants.  The final evacuation order caught you in the middle of your 16 hour extended shift, and you barely managed to escape the building, tools in hand.
data.name: CBM Engineer

data.description: You were one of the chief executives of the Megacorporation Wraitheon Robotics ltd.  and consistently ranked among the most powerful and influential persons of the world.  Still, the apocalypse has caught you unprepared, and now after the dissolution of your company and the crumbling of your network of influence, your were left powerless and destitute, and must rely on your two robotic bodyguards to survive.
data.name: Wraitheon Executive

data.description: Before all this, you were an 'independent contractor', a spy and mercenary at the service of megacorporations.  Using highly experimental holographic technology, you once took on impossible jobs one after the other.
data.name: Corporate Infiltrator

data.description: Through a lifetime of hard work, dedication, boot-licking and immense luck, you achieved the almost impossible, and were promoted from an indentured clerk to a minor managerial position.  Although you were still an expendable peon among the ranks of the corporation, you earned numbers most people could only dream of, and you savored it.
data.name: Assistant Sub-Executive

data.description: You are an uplifted elephant hybrid, a precisely engineered enforcer creature created by combining human, elephant and mastodon DNA.  Unlike most of your war-minded kin, you sought a peaceful retirement after completing your compulsive service period, and even earned the right to be considered a full citizen.  Now, with the end of the world in the horizon, it seems that your combat skills might come useful once again.
data.name: Uplifted Enforcer

data.description: You worked as a mercenary across six continents for a dozen corps.  A VP at the last corp decided he wanted to put you on retainer and you agreed to a three month gig in return for some additional bionics.  You woke up with an extra bionic, a cranial bomb that needed to be reset every month or so or it blows up.  Now you're free until the bomb goes off.  Maybe you'll find someone who can remove it.
data.name: Bionic Operator

data.description: Heavy stun and knockback, 1.5x bash damage, crit only
messages: You smash %s with a pressurized slam
<npcname> smashes %s with a pressurized slam
data.name: Pressure Crunch

data.//: TODO: use copy-from inheritance for solarpack uncrafts -- it is currently not working properly #24702
TODO: use copy-from inheritance for solarpack uncrafts -- it is currently not working properly #24702
Conversion of a normal smartphone into an atomic one by replacing the power supply.
Google tells me that golf clubs often have titanium heads, so that's a source of it.

data.description: During the riots and chaos, you hid in a robot dispatch center hoping the robots would protect you, but they may prove more dangerous than the zombies.
data.name: Robots

data.sound: "I have no mouth and yet I scream!"
data.speaker: afs_mon_migoturret

data.sound: "The better to eat you!  I don't actually think I eat anymore."
data.speaker: afs_mon_migoturret

data.sound: "Cyrus said if I was patient, he could get me a new body."
data.speaker: afs_mon_migoturret

data.sound: "I can't keep myself from killing you, please run!"
data.speaker: afs_mon_migoturret

data.description: Creates several short-lived holograms around you.
Creates a short-lived hologram.
Causes an existing hologram to explode into burning light, harming and stunning enemies close to it.
Causes an existing hologram to explode into burning light, harming and stunning enemies close to it.
Allows you to swap places with a previously existing holographic image of yourself.  If the universe itself can't tell you apart, who could?
Grants you an extra turn.
data.name: Aftershock Extra Turn

data.description: Grants you a stackable speed bonus.
data.name: Aftershock Stacking Speed Bonus

data.description: A sturdy, lightweight frame made from titanium.  Useful for crafting.
A sturdy, lightweight foldable frame made from titanium.

From the makers of the best-selling* FOODCO kitchen buddy comes the MetalMaster forge buddy, for all your metalworking, firing, and welding needs!  It's just as clunky and awkward as the thing it's spinning off, and still requires a vehicle battery to function.

Because it *clearly* needed one, this large all-in-one station provides the functions of FOODCO's kitchen buddy, now with complementary fume hoods and chemistry materials.  Why a chef would need a chemical rig is anyone's guess, but you can mount it on a vehicle to make use of it.
Skillet, pot, hotplate, and chemistry set; everything you need to cook food and chemicals.  Includes proper fume vents and a separator, so you don't contaminate your food with toxic chemicals.
A large and complex piloting station from a military vehicle, including a camera station, steering tools, and electronics controls.

This solar panel is obviously cutting-edge technology and given where you found it, should probably provide a LOT of power.  It's covered in strange-looking material, but the covering looks rather fragile; it doesn't look like it could support a reinforcing sheet, either.
data.name: quantum solar panel

data.//: For cs_car_showroom
grenade launcher version
data.name: Armored Personnel Carrier

data.description: This is little more than a sheetmetal basin welded to the bottom of the vehicle, extending below it.  Though it holds a lot of stuff, its shoddiness makes it fragile.
A piece of sheet metal welded onto a vehicle as armor.  Thin, and not as protective as proper armor, but it'll do if there's nothing else available.
A medium-sized household refrigerator, converted to utilize vehicle power.  When turned on, food inside will be cooled, extending the time it has before spoiling.
A medium-sized glass door refrigerator, converted to utilize vehicle power.  When turned on, food inside will be cooled, extending the time it has before spoiling.
A medium sized commercial freezer, converted to run on vehicle power.  When turned on, food inside will be frozen, completely stopping it from spoiling.
A medium sized glass door freezer, converted to run on vehicle power.  When turned on, food inside will be frozen, completely stopping it from spoiling.
A welding and metalworking station, powered by the vehicle's battery.  'e'xamine the forge rig to utilize its welder or soldering iron; you'll still need glare protection.
An all-in-one kitchen unit and chemistry lab.  'e'xamine it to use its hotplate to heat up food or drinks.
An all-in-one kitchen unit, chemistry lab, and food preparation area.  'e'xamine it to use its hotplate to heat up food or drinks.
A large and complex dashboard mounted with steering controls and several LCD touch screens for controlling vehicle systems.
An ultralight titanium framework.  Other vehicle components can be mounted on it, and it can be attached to other frames to increase the vehicle's size.
An ultralight titanium foldable framework.  Other vehicle components can be mounted on it, and it can be attached to other frames to increase the vehicle's size.
An extremely high performance solar panel.  Will recharge the vehicle's electrical power when exposed to the sun.  Clouds will slow the recharge speed.  Extremely fragile and cannot be armored.
data.name: quantum solar panel

data.description: A cargo space for carrying robots.  'e'xamine it to capture a robot next to you, or to release the robot currently contained.  When selecting a robot to capture, choose its tile relative to you, not the part.
data.name: robot carrier

info.description: Changes the overmap to be more readable.  Buildings are color coded by type and use initial letter of their names instead of ^v<>.
info.name: Alternative Map Key

info.description: Core content for Cataclysm-BN
info.name: Bright Nights

info.description: Enables the bionic slots system, which limits the number of CBMs you can install in each bodypart.
info.name: Bionic Slots

info.description: Hairdressing tools can now actually change your hairstyle! Change your hairstyle and your mood in the Cataclysm!
info.name: Change Hairstyle

info.description: Adds a few civilian monsters & a faction that simulates the collapse of civilization and the resulting riots.
info.name: Civilians

data.//: They are human after all
data.description: This hysterical individual has a faint, yet fading, glint of intelligence in their bloodshot eyes.  They sometimes scream in fear and behave clearly irrationally.  This will quite likely cause their demise at the hands of the undead horde rather quickly.

data.//: Will not be able to do this in their current mental state:
data.description: They just stand there, staring into the void, with a vacant expression on their face.  Barely moving, they will probably fall to the undead horde soon.

data.//: Copied from zombie cop
data.//2: Uses a baton for melee attacks
data.description: Gun in hand, this police officer is determined to make a last stand against the horde.
Obviously, this aggravated yet rallied person has lost their wits and tries to fight the undead with just a knife in their hands.

data.//: So they are not perpetually fleeing despite lying in a hospital bed
data.description: This person is severely ill in some form or another, requiring intensive care that is not available anymore after the Cataclysm.  There is nothing you can do for them.
Obsessed with finding their offspring, they scour the environment for certain death.
Obsessed with finding their parent, they scour the environment for certain death.
A gun carried by overconfident officer monsters, more inaccurate for balancing reasons.

data.job_description: Ready to fight.

data.description: The cataclysm may have caught you off guard, but you're not going down without a fight!
data.name: Futile Fighter

data.description: You and a few neighbors grabbed whatever sticks and scraps you could find. Now the cataclysm is here, and you’re a futile fighter in a makeshift militia - badly equipped, badly trained, but determined to go down swinging.
data.name: Makeshift Militia

info.description: (Formerly Classic Zombies): Only spawn classic zombies (normal zombies and zombie animals) and natural wildlife.  Zombies do not revive and do not need pulping.  This disables certain buildings and map extras.  The long term plan is to make this a classic zombie movie simulator set in the present day.  If you have this mod enabled in experimental, some items may disappear.
info.name: Dark Days of the Dead

info.description: Want a little crazy in your Cataclysm?  Try this one.
info.name: Crazy Cataclysm

data.description: Seems like some kind of Genetic Lifeform and Disk Operating System, attached to a potato.
The hit cookie from Mycus Industries!  Now comes in addicting flavors such as Marloss, Chanterelle, and The Encroaching, Unavoidable Death of Human Civilization.
Attached to this grenade is a tag with 'debug' written on it.  Does not seem to work like a grenade, handle with care.
Attached to this grenade is a tag with 'debug' written on it.  Does not seem to work like a grenade, handle with care.  Better throw it!

A powerful ion energy generator is implanted on your chest.  Fires a powerful, ever-expanding energy blast.  The resulting blast ignites oxygen creating fires as it moves and an explosion on impact.  Close range use is highly discouraged.

A powerful ion energy generator is implanted on your chest.  Fires a powerful, ever-expanding energy blast.  The resulting blast ignites oxygen creating fires as it moves and an explosion on impact.  Close range use is highly discouraged.

A powerful ion energy generator is implanted on your chest.  Fires a powerful, ever-expanding energy blast that goes through various targets.  The energy shot ignites oxygen creating fires as it moves and an explosion on impact.
You can YEET all the bullets with this baby.  YEET them right into all of the zombies in your path, all the hulks, the spiders, and those damned mole rats.

data.//: Autumn is halloween
data.name: GROUP_ZOMBIE

data.//: Additional bears during winter, when they hibernate.
data.name: GROUP_CAVE

data.description: A human body with pale blue flesh, crackling with electrical energy.  It seems eager to tell you something.
Devoid entirely of flesh and organs, this walking skeleton rattles you to the bone.  In its bony hand, it holds a pristine trumpet, unmarred by the end of the world.
A lesser skeleton, raised by the forlorn dooting of a trumpet.
A smoking husk is all that remains of this once proud bear.  Its black eyes gaze at you with malice… and hunger.

data.sound: When the testing is over, you will be missed.
data.speaker: aperture_potato

data.sound: Unbelievable.  You, [subject name here], must be the pride of [subject hometown here]!
data.speaker: aperture_potato

data.sound: That thing is probably some sort of raw sewage container.  Go ahead and rub your face all over it.
data.speaker: aperture_potato

data.sound: For your own safety and the safety of others, please refrain from touching [bzzzzzt]
data.speaker: aperture_potato

data.sound: No one will blame you for giving up.  In fact, quitting at this point is a perfectly reasonable response.
data.speaker: aperture_potato

data.sound: Fantastic!  You remained resolute and resourceful in an atmosphere of extreme pessimism.
data.speaker: aperture_potato

data.sound: Hello again.  To reiterate our previous warning: [garbled]
data.speaker: aperture_potato

data.sound: If you become light headed from thirst, feel free to pass out.
data.speaker: aperture_potato

data.sound: No further compliance information is required or will be provided, and you are an excellent test subject!
data.speaker: aperture_potato

data.sound: Thank you for helping us help you help us all.
data.speaker: aperture_potato

data.sound: Cake and grief counseling will be available at the conclusion of the test.
data.speaker: aperture_potato

data.sound: The experiment is nearing its conclusion.
data.speaker: aperture_potato

data.sound: The Enrichment Center is required to remind you that you will be baked [garbled] cake.
data.speaker: aperture_potato

data.sound: We are very, very happy for your success.
data.speaker: aperture_potato

data.sound: We are throwing a party in honor of your tremendous success.
data.speaker: aperture_potato

data.sound: A party associate will arrive shortly to collect you for your party.
data.speaker: aperture_potato

data.sound: I know you're there.  I can feel you here.
data.speaker: aperture_potato

data.sound: You're not going the right way.
data.speaker: aperture_potato

data.sound: You shouldn't be here.  This isn't safe for you.
data.speaker: aperture_potato

data.sound: Maybe you think you're helping yourself.  But you're not.  This isn't helping anyone.
data.speaker: aperture_potato

data.sound: It's not too late to turn back.
data.speaker: aperture_potato

data.sound: Someone is going to get badly hurt.
data.speaker: aperture_potato

data.sound: Uh oh.  Somebody cut the cake.  I told them to wait for you, but they did it anyway.
data.speaker: aperture_potato

data.sound: This is your fault.  It didn't have to be like this.
data.speaker: aperture_potato

data.sound: You're not a good person.  You know that, right?
data.speaker: aperture_potato

data.sound: Good people don't end up here.
data.speaker: aperture_potato

data.sound: You should have turned left before.
data.speaker: aperture_potato

data.sound: Despite your violent behavior, the only thing you've managed to break so far is my heart.
data.speaker: aperture_potato

data.sound: What was that?  Did you say something?
data.speaker: aperture_potato

data.sound: I'd just like to point out that you were given every opportunity to succeed.
data.speaker: aperture_potato

data.sound: You've been wrong about every single thing you've ever done, including this thing.
data.speaker: aperture_potato

data.sound: I have an infinite capacity for knowledge, and even I'm not sure what's going on outside.
data.speaker: aperture_potato

data.sound: Stop squirming and die like an adult or I'm going to delete your backup.
data.speaker: aperture_potato

data.sound: Good job on that, by the way.  SARCASM SPHERE SELF-TEST COMPLETE.
data.speaker: aperture_potato

data.sound: I don't want to tell you your business, but if it were me, I'd leave that thing alone.
data.speaker: aperture_potato

data.sound: Let's be honest: Neither one of us knows what that thing does.  Just put it in the corner, and I'll deal with it later.
data.speaker: aperture_potato

data.sound: I know you don't believe this, but everything that has happened so far was for your benefit.
data.speaker: aperture_potato

data.sound: I'm checking some blueprints, and I think…  Yes, right here.  You're definitely going the wrong way.
data.speaker: aperture_potato

data.sound: Are you even listening to me?
data.speaker: aperture_potato

THE TRUTH ABOUT CATACLYSM'S DEVELOPMENT - AND ITS DISGUSTING SECRET
