20 EASY WAYS TO ESCAPE A LAB - #4 WILL BLOW YOUR MIND

data.sound: "I've got a feeling we're not in Kansas anymore."
data.speaker: mon_mi_go

data.sound: "Then who the hell else are you talkin' to?"
data.speaker: mon_mi_go

data.sound: "Who the fuck do you think you're talking to?"
data.speaker: mon_mi_go

data.sound: "What we've got here is failure to communicate."
data.speaker: mon_mi_go

data.sound: "I'm as mad as hell, and I'm not going to take this anymore!"
data.speaker: mon_mi_go

data.sound: "We don't need no badges!  I don't have to show you any stinking badges!"
data.speaker: mon_mi_go

data.sound: "They may take our lives, but they'll never take our freedom!"
data.speaker: mon_mi_go

data.sound: "If you let my daughter go now, that'll be the end of it.  I will not look for you, I will not pursue you."
data.speaker: mon_mi_go

data.sound: "If you don't, I will look for you, I will find you, and I will kill you."
data.speaker: mon_mi_go

data.sound: "Roads?  Where we're going, we don't need roads."
data.speaker: mon_mi_go

data.sound: "Fasten your seatbelts.  It's going to be a bumpy night."
data.speaker: mon_mi_go

data.sound: "You've got to ask yourself one question:  'Do I feel lucky?'  Well, do ya punk?"
data.speaker: mon_mi_go

data.sound: "The first rule of The Lab is:  You do not talk about The Lab."
data.speaker: mon_mi_go

data.sound: "Don't be afraid.  It's just a big, big lap dog."
data.speaker: mon_mi_go

data.sound: "I'm going to make him an offer he can't refuse."
data.speaker: mon_mi_go

data.sound: "This is the beginning of a beautiful friendship."
data.speaker: mon_mi_go

data.sound: "If you built it, he will come."
data.speaker: mon_mi_go

data.sound: "Frankly, my dear, I don't give a damn."
data.speaker: mon_mi_go

data.sound: "All work and no play makes Jack a dull boy."
data.speaker: mon_mi_go

data.sound: "Remember: all I'm offering is the truth.  Nothing more."
data.speaker: mon_mi_go

data.sound: "I have had it with these motherfucking snakes!"
data.speaker: mon_mi_go

data.sound: "Back up, put down the gun, and gimme some tropical flavored bubblicious."
data.speaker: mon_mi_go

data.sound: "Bullshit!  You didn't convince me!  Show me your real war face!"
data.speaker: mon_mi_go

data.sound: "I saw it.  It was an accident."
data.speaker: mon_turret_light

data.sound: "But I need to protect the humans!"
data.speaker: mon_turret_light

data.sound: "Thought you'd seen the last of us, didn't ya?"
data.speaker: mon_turret_light

data.sound: "What's a guy gotta got to do to get some bullets around here?"
data.speaker: mon_turret_light

data.sound: "I can't see a thing.  What just happened?  Better open fire!  [click click click click]  Dang!"
data.speaker: mon_turret_light

data.sound: "Hey, hold on now WHOA WHOA WHOA!"
data.speaker: mon_turret_light

data.sound: "Hey!  Squeaky-voice!  Gimme some of your bullets!"
data.speaker: mon_turret_light

data.sound: "Can I get some bullets here?  Anybody got bullets?"
data.speaker: mon_turret_light

data.sound: "Shootin' blanks every time, ALL the time."
data.speaker: mon_turret_light

data.sound: "Watch and learn, everybody.  Watch and learn."
data.speaker: mon_turret_light

data.sound: "So what am I, uh, supposed to do here?"
data.speaker: mon_turret_light

info.description: Adds the Catastrophe Response and Investigation Team (C.R.I.T.), with includes additional professions, guns/mods, WIP enemies, mutations, martial arts, melee weapons, and some QOL changes.
info.name: <color_red>C.R.I.T. Expansion</color>

data.description: A cheap mish mash of planks and sticks with a log pillar that keeps it together.  It is capable of supporting an upper level or roof.  Dirt and stones make the wall secure.  Somewhat flammable.
data.name: stick wall

data.description: A round tin of small light grain .177 lead pellets.  These are common, tipped field pellets that can deal some light damage but are generally used for plinking.
data.name: .117 lead pellets

data.description: A round tin of small light grain .177 steel pellets.  These are common, tipped field pellets that can deal some light damage but are generally used for plinking.
data.name: .117 steel pellets

data.description: A round tin of small light grain .177 superalloy pellets.  These are common, tipped field pellets that can deal some light damage but are generally used for plinking.
data.name: .117 superalloy pellets

data.description: A stable, heavier grain lead pellet with the purpose of expanding upon hitting a target for maximized damage, the dome shape allows it to pack quite a punch for something so small
data.name: .117 domed HP pellets

data.//: Based off of the Gamo Redfire pellet, the plastic tip pushes back into the lead pleet to cause a greater deformation and ballooning effect; makes birds explode!
data.description: A medium grain lead pellet tipped with a pointed bit of hard plastic with the purpose of maximum expansion upon hitting a target.
data.name: .117 tipped HP pellets

data.description: An gimmicky alloy pellet with the purpose of reaching a higher velocity than a normal lead pellet for breaking the sound barrier resulting in an extremely loud crack, not so useful for stealth.
data.name: .117 alloy pellets

data.description: A helical magazine of hollow-point alloy bullets propelled by pockets of primer.  Not the most lethal thing out there, but it still packs a punch without the worry of having a stray shot seriously damaging the environment.
data.name: pulse round

data.description: This is the C.R.I.T. standard issue face mask, lined with Kevlar for extra protection.  A few filters provide decent environmental safety, but it was not intended for extended use.  It has a basic integrated HUD.
data.name: C.R.I.T. face mask

data.description: C.R.I.T. standard-issue boots.  Next-gen gels keep feet comfortable and hygienic during long-term missions while absorbing shock and heat from outside-sources.  Superalloy mesh and rubber offer quite a bit of chemical protection as well.  Decently heavy though

C.R.I.T. skeletonized boots.  Based off of C.R.I.T. boots, the light-armor variant was created for missions in warmer climates.  The light-armor boots keep most of the old features of the standard issue boots but trade in protection for easier movement.
C.R.I.T. standard-issue gloves.  Made with superalloy mesh for those with gene-modding and/or mutations while still allowing greater manipulation of items and moderate protection.

C.R.I.T. standard-issue glove liners.  Made with neoprene and rubber mesh for warmth and fingertip-less for those with gene-modding and/or mutations while still allowing greater manipulation of items and moderate protection.

C.R.I.T. standard-issue pack.  Based on the MOLLE backpack's design, this smaller pack strikes a fine balance between storage space and encumbrance and allows multiple larger weapons to be holstered, drawing and holstering is still rather awkward even with the magnetized clips, but practice helps.
data.name: C.R.I.T. backpack

data.description: C.R.I.T. standard-issue chestrig, has mesh and MOLLE loops for gear and slots with light-armor padding.
data.name: C.R.I.T. chestrig

data.description: C.R.I.T. standard-issue leg armor.  Simple design and durable material allows for easy movement and the padding keeps the legs safe and warm in colder conditions.
A pair of arm guards made from superalloy molded upon neoprene, and then insulated with rubber.  They are sturdy and will block attacks, but they are ridiculously heavy.
C.R.I.T. standard-issue belt.  Keeps your trousers up and your weapons on your hip.
data.name: C.R.I.T. web belt

data.description: A thick full-length duster coat with rubber insulation.  More than mildly encumbering, but rather protective against any anti-infantry electrical discharges from the robots.  Has several pockets for storage.
data.name: C.R.I.T. infantry duster

data.description: C.R.I.T. standard issue General Engineering Assistance Rig.  Plugged into your spinal cord, this device improves your overall physique and provides basic information on your surroundings.
data.name: C.R.I.T. S-I G.E.A.R

data.description: An airtight, flexible suit of woven composite fibers complete with segmented plates of armor.  A complex system digitizes items in an individual pocket universe for storage while built in joint-torsion ratchets generate the necessary energy required to power the interface.
data.name: C.R.I.T. Engineering Suit

data.description: A relatively simple suit of armor.  A suit of woven composite fibers combined with a cleansuit core and strategically placed segmented Kevlar plates keep the suit light-weight and the one wearing it alive while offering superb resistance to the elements and ambient radiation.
data.name: C.R.I.T. Armored Anomaly Suit

data.description: A set of pouches that can be worn on the thighs using buckled straps.  This variety is more compact and is favored by the C.R.I.T. for its ease of use.

A series of plates, guards and buckles which assemble into a suit of sturdy body-armor which usually goes over other armor.  Overlapping steel plates on top of Kevlar plates cover vast expanses as the armor juts off in places so it can deflect attacks.  Built with the idea that comfort is less important than safety, this heavy suit is difficult to move about in but highly protective.  Various adjustable connectors such as straps and clips hold it together.

C.R.I.T. Enforcer boots.  Metal plates vaguely molded into the shape of oversized feet which clamp down onto your own footwear keep your feet out of harms way.  It looks terrible and feels clunky unlike most of C.R.I.T.'s designs, but they do seem to be worth using if you were to be in the middle of a warzone.

A suit of modern body-armor.  Strategically placed superalloy plates keep the suit's weight minimal while Kevlar plates other areas and a lining of soft neoprene pads areas for extra comfort.  Most importantly, this can be worn comfortably under other armor.
data.name: C.R.I.T. Soldier Suit

data.description: A matte black suit of outdated and bulky looking plate armor fitted onto a soft Kevlar body-suit.  Retrofitted with new armor improvements, this heavy armor will definitely protect you from practically anything.  Just make sure you can actually walk with it on though.
data.name: C.R.I.T. Lone Wolf Series Armor

data.description: C.R.I.T. standard-issue blouse.  Durable, lightweight, and has ample storage.  Super-flex neoprene keeps one warm in moderately cold weather while a sleek design keeps it from being too flashy.  A zipper at the back and front allows for quick donning and doffing.
data.name: C.R.I.T. blouse

data.description: C.R.I.T. standard-issue trousers.  Durable, lightweight and has ample storage.  Super-flex neoprene keeps one warm in moderately cold weather.
C.R.I.T. dress pants.  A minimalist sleek design makes the pants lightweight and it offers ok pockets.  Super-flex neoprene keeps one warm in moderately cold weather.
C.R.I.T. standard-issue helmet liner.  Keeps the noggin warm.
data.name: C.R.I.T. helmet liner

data.description: A sleek pair of dress shoes.  Fancy but easy on the eyes.
C.R.I.T. standard-issue rec gloves.  Skin-hugging and sleek, these gloves are made with cotton with a neoprene lining for grip-pads and warmth.
A waterproofed full-length duster coat.  Made with neoprene, comfort and functionality meet together to form a fancy but sleek contemporary design.  It has several pockets for storage.
data.name: C.R.I.T. rec duster

data.description: Functionality meets fashion in this waterproofed C.R.I.T. standard issue rec cover.  Thick enough to provide warmth in colder weather, this hat shares the same sleek design of most of C.R.I.T.'s gear.
data.name: C.R.I.T. rec hat

data.description: A bundle of plant matter
data.name: withered plant bundle

data.description: Experimental low power sidearm under development in C.R.I.T. R&D, the .5 LP is a relatively weak but accurate laser pistol.  The double-barrel design compensates for the lack of raw power and yet the gun manages to be relatively easy to aim and lightweight due to the superalloy construction. It was broken during heavy use while escaping the apocalypse, perhaps you could repair it
data.name: Broken C.R.I.T. .5 LP

data.description: Experimental low power sidearm under development in C.R.I.T. R&D, the .5 LP is a relatively weak but accurate laser pistol.  The double-barrel design compensates for the lack of raw power and yet the gun manages to be relatively easy to aim and lightweight due to the superalloy construction.
data.name: C.R.I.T. .5 LP

data.description: A tried and true favorite from the bowels of R&D hell.  Based off of a researcher's video on three taped-together .5 LPs on a hand held power drill, this gun is a relatively light weapon for the amount of UPS it eats and destruction it can cause.
data.name: C.R.I.T. Chain Laser

data.description: A short-barrel lightweight laser gun developed by C.R.I.T. R&D.  Mainly developed to test out a new breakthrough in laser weapons.
data.name: C.R.I.T. Laser Carbine

data.description: A heavy energy gun developed by C.R.I.T. R&D.  Mainly developed to test out a new breakthrough in hybrid weaponry.
data.name: C.R.I.T. Energy Rifle

data.description: A simple combination gun.  This military-grade semi-auto carbine couples the mid-range versatility of the 9mm with the power of the 12 gauge shotgun.  To further compliment the CQB aspect, the stock is built to amplify the user's force and the rugged construction with tonfa-like grip can handle bashing in enemy heads.  An integrated magazine makes it a pain to reload, but keeps your clip from ejecting accidently.
data.name: C.R.I.T. CQB Standard Issue

data.description: Experimental CQB weapon system under development in C.R.I.T. R&D.
data.name: C.R.I.T. Fire Glove

data.//: You can get a decent Ruger .177 at walmart for $99
data.description: A surprisingly powerful airgun that can reliably hunt small game.  The small lead or alloy pellets that can be chambered provide it decent power in every shot.  It's fairly accurate and can be somewhat as damaging as a .22 short, but the break action charging system requires some arm strength to load a pellet.
data.name: pellet gun

data.description: A surprisingly powerful airgun that can reliably hunt most game.  The small lead or alloy pellets that can be chambered provide it decent power in every shot.  It's fairly accurate and can be somewhat as damaging as a .22 long, but the break action charging system requires some arm strength to load a pellet.
data.name: high power pellet gun

data.description: Experimental cutting tool under development in C.R.I.T. R&D.  It fires an extremely hot wave of plasma that slices into materials.
data.name: C.R.I.T. Plasma Cutter

data.description: Experimental double purpose tool under development in C.R.I.T. R&D.  It takes a regular nail and then elongates it within a fraction of a second before firing it out, upon reaching a target, the fragile stake explodes into shards.
data.name: C.R.I.T. Rivet Driver

data.description: Experimental high power cutting tool under development in C.R.I.T. R&D.  It fires plasma in a wide line for slicing into dense materials.
data.name: C.R.I.T. Line Gun

data.description: Experimental tri-barrel sub-sonic rifle under development in C.R.I.T. R&D.  Great for enclosed spaces and mobs of enemies.  Shoots alloy rounds which instantly mushroom out upon impact.
data.name: C.R.I.T. Pulse Rifle

data.description: Experimental EM saw under development in C.R.I.T. R&D.  Great for distance cutting of material.
data.name: C.R.I.T. Ripper

data.description: A small barrel which launches signal flares.  However, due to its awkward position, it has lower accuracy compared to an actual flaregun.
data.name: Underslung flare launcher

data.description: The integrated underbarrel shotgun of this gun which holds a single shot.  It's irremovable.
data.name: CQB SI shotgun

data.description: A military-grade stock which folds reducing the guns volume.  The weight and the pivoting hook which latches onto your forearm allows for greater stability.
data.name: butt hook stock

data.description: A set of optics made to fit on laser weapons, which will diffract the laser beam into several lower powered beams.  This slightly increases point-blank damage and makes it difficult to not hit, but reduces range.

data.//: C.R.I.T. Science lab set
Common gunmods typically owned by citizens and found in many locations.
Military specification gunmods found only at military sites.
Assorted factory crafted energy weapons.
Military specification rifles only ever found at military sites.
Standard (non-winter) set of clothes worn by soldiers and paramilitary forces.
Winter set of clothes worn by soldiers and paramilitary forces.

data.description: This is the C.R.I.T. Spec Ops modified gasmask, fitted with top-of-the-line electronics and lined with Kevlar for extra protection in order to keep one's head where it should be.  Various filters and other high tech wizardry allow for enhanced oxygen intake and safety even under bombardment.  It has an integrated HUD and the option to turn it on for more features.

use_action.msg: C.R.T HUD booting up…
use_action.need_charges_msg: Power levels too low for safe boot up

data.description: This is the C.R.I.T. Spec Ops modified gasmask.  It is currently on and draining power for the HUD, low-level nightvision and other protective elements.

use_action.msg: C.R.T HUD deactivating.

data.description: The C.R.I.T. Spec Ops Enhanced Movement vest is embedded with high-tech filaments and reactive servos which protects its wearer and assists in movement at the cost high of power usage.  It is commonly worn by C.R.I.T. Spec Ops for its ease of use and maneuverability.  Turn it on for suit mode, extra protection and movement.

use_action.msg: C.R.I.T. EM booting up…
use_action.need_charges_msg: Power levels too low for safe bootup…

data.description: The C.R.I.T. Spec Ops Enhanced Movement vest is embedded with high-tech filaments, reactive servos and a generator which pumps a crystallized liquid that protects its wearer from most heavy combat situations at the cost of extreme power usage.  It is commonly worn by C.R.I.T. Spec Ops.  This vest is currently in suit form and draining your UPS power at high rates.

use_action.msg: C.R.I.T. E.M powering off…

data.description: C.R.T standard-issue helmet.  Protects the noggin and has a stretch of insulated steel mesh for neck warmth and protection.

use_action.msg: You turn the %s on.

data.description: C.R.I.T. standard-issue helmet.  Protects the noggin and has a stretch of insulated steel mesh for neck warmth and protection.  A tactically dim flashlight is attached to the side.  This light is currently on and drawing power.

use_action.msg: You turn the %s off.

data.description: A simple, durable steel canteen that can heat up food with built in plutonium heating elements.
data.name: C.R.I.T. canteen

data.description: C.R.I.T special-issue pack. This is based on the MOLLE backpack's design, but comes with a integrated UPS and a few protected but interwoven solar cells
data.name: C.R.I.T solarpack

recharge_scheme.//: Recharge 0.025 Kilojoule per second, sun produces ~680 W/m^2, so it realistic for protected panels

data.description: C.R.I.T. standard-issue med kit designed for repeated use. Uses photosynthesis to slowly regenerate the integrated antiseptic based on cattail jelly.
data.name: C.R.I.T. med kit

data.description: C.R.I.T. standard-issue mess kit designed for ease of transport.  Based off of the normal military mess kit, but made to be telescopic, the parts are made from a thin sheet of a stainless superalloy composite and are insulated with ceramic.  Sadly, this compact reimagining loses much of its battery life but does have a rather small (useless) solar panel installed.  Also comes with an absurdly small integrated fpoon and knife spatula set!
data.name: C.R.I.T. mess kit

data.description: C.R.I.T. standard-issue knife.  Has a knuckleduster guard and a small, hooked pry bar at the bottom for opening simple things and bashing in heads.  Matte black finish helps it avoid flash in dim-light situations and tanto tip allows for light-armor penetration.  Blade length allows for decent reach.
C.R.I.T. CQB knuckledusters.  Not too different from any normal pair other than weight.
C.R.I.T. melee weapon.  Alien runes adorn the carbon steel blade.  The blade seems dull at first glance, and yet upon closer observation, a hum of energy thrums from within.
An aura of bloodlust hovers around you.  Something about the power of this blade makes it seem WAY faster in your hands than it has any right to be.
This exists only to trigger on-self effects from an on-hit action.
This creates the effect that heals the caster.

C.R.I.T. R&D's masterpiece weapon.  Alien runes adorn the ridiculously oversized carbon steel blade and a hum of energy thrums from within.  Merely brushing your fingers over the weapon brings a feeling of invincibility.  It looks more like a raw heap of iron than a sword.  The thing is… can you wield it?

hit_you_effect.message: The runes on your blade glow as you carve into %2$s.
hit_you_effect.npc_message: The runes on %1$s's blade glow as they carve into %2$s.

data.description: An incredibly sharp, heavy duty, one-handed hatchet.  Makes a great melee weapon, and is useful both for chopping things and for use as a hammer when extended.
data.name: C.R.I.T hatchet

use_action.msg: You extend your hatchet

data.description: An incredibly sharp, heavy duty, full length axe.  Makes a heavy-hitting melee weapon, and is useful both for chopping things and for use as a hammer when extended.
data.name: C.R.I.T axe

use_action.msg: You collapse your axe

data.description: C.R.I.T. standard-issue collapsible spade.  A built in vibration system that is powered by the user's movement allows the smaller spade to clear soil like a larger shovel.
data.name: C.R.I.T. entrenching tool

data.description: C.R.I.T. standard issue guard tonfa.  The length allows for great reach and the domed tip allows for greater impact than a cylinder style baton.
data.name: C.R.I.T. night stick

data.//: C.R.I.T. lab

data.description: An advanced military manual on C.R.I.T Blade-work.
An offensive style that is centered around rapid slashes and prodding.  Each attack landed increases combat ability
initiate: Initiate blade-work.
%s initiates blade-work.
data.name: C.R.I.T Blade-work

onhit_buffs.description: Additional DMG, and Arpen per stack.  Max of 5 stacks
onhit_buffs.name: C.R.I.T Intensity

static_buffs.description: Increased accuracy along with minor cut and stab damage with piercing capability.
static_buffs.name: C.R.I.T Calculation

data.description: An advanced military manual on C.R.I.T Enforcer melee.
A defensive style that is centered around stunning swings, knockback and grounding enemies.  Each attack landed increases your armor by 0.125 and offers other combat bonuses based on stats.
initiate: You ready yourself to stand your ground.
%s draws a line in the sand.
data.name: C.R.I.T Enforcement

onhit_buffs.description: +0.05 armor and other small bonuses per stack.  Max of 10 stacks
onhit_buffs.name: C.R.I.T Buildup

static_buffs.name: C.R.I.T Guard

<npcname> swiftly jabs their weapon into %s

<npcname> releases a crushing blow at %s

data.description: An advanced military manual on C.R.I.T general CQB.
You have received defensive training.  For every hit you land, gain various miniscule combat bonuses that scale off of your stats.
data.name: C.R.I.T Melee Training

data.description: A style centered around rapid strikes and piercing jabs.  Each attack landed adds a plethora of combat bonuses.  25 percent bash damage.
initiate: Initiate CQB.
%s initiates CQB.
data.name: C.R.I.T CQB

onhit_buffs.description: +Atk Speed and other small bonuses based on DEX per stack.  Max of 5 stacks
onhit_buffs.name: C.R.I.T Tenacity

static_buffs.description: DEX provides dodge ability, accuracy and minor cut / stab damage with slight piercing capability.  50 Percent Bash Damage.
static_buffs.name: C.R.I.T Initiative

data.description: A horrifically twisted human body.  Two massive, bladed appendages have burst through its shoulders where they are poised above its head as it stalks about with terrifying purpose.
data.name: Slasher Necromorph

data.description: A horrifically mutilated human body.  Two scrawny blades have freshly bursted through its hand to deal with prey and the haunting visage of a jawless maw and a gaping wound in its forehead sends chills down your spine.  The awkward steps it takes slows it down greatly.
data.name: Weak Slasher Necromorph

data.description: Clad in heavy assault gear, an eerie light green glows beneath its helmet from sunken eye sockets and a gaping mouth.  Strange blade like points have burst out of it's forearms making it a formidable force to be reckoned with.
data.name: Waster Necromorph

data.description: This once-human body is barely recognizable, scrambling about on its abdomen as it leaps forward with immense arm strength.  With elongated fangs that can easily mutilate your flesh, the grotesque face roars incessantly.  The lower body has fused together into one giant tail with a barbed spike.
data.name: Leaper Necromorph

data.description: With narrow blades coming out of its hands, this corpse spasmically dashes to-and-fro with surprising speed.  It carries itself quite steadily when idle, further observation shows that the person before this husk was a C.R.I.T S-I G.E.A.R operator.
data.name: Twitcher Necromorph

data.//: no GUILT because it no longer looks enough like a child to evoke pity
data.description: A shrieking mutated child zombie.  The face is is mainly blank with eyes swollen shut and a torn-open mouth with flaps of flesh hanging to the side.  A pair of seemingly purposeless appendages sprout from its shoulders before ending in its arms.  Its small hands end in sharp claws.
data.name: Pack Necromorph

data.description: A rather mutilated corpse covered in gaping sores.  Hanging arms with hands that have long corroded away reveal jagged edges that could easily pierce into your flesh.  A sticky, frothing yellow sludge flows from its exposed internal organs to its unhinged jaw where it drips, hissing as it eats through material.
data.name: Puker Necromorph

data.//: Mostly scientists, lab personnel and regular zombies.
data.name: GROUP_LAB_FEMA

data.description: A dismembered arm that slowly crawls forward.  Occasionally, tentacles sprout out from the wound and lash about wildly.
data.name: Animate Arm

data.description: A headless humanoid that slowly sways.  Ornate and functional armor adorn this dreadful corpse which carries itself with an unerringly terrible steadiness.  A long tentacle has sprouted out of its right arm which occasionally flails about wildly.
data.name: Dullahan

data.description: The light around you bends strangely, making it harder for enemies to notice you.
data.name: Shadow Meld

data.description: Aside from your appearances, your movements are incredibly graceful and allow you to seemingly glide through every task.
data.name: Moon-lit Grace

data.description: You eyes are a pleasant shade of hypnotic scarlet.  People feel mildly persuaded by you.
data.name: Red Iris

data.//: Parallel reference to Woken Furies quote from the Altered Carbon series and song.
data.description: Emerge from the grave of the old world, and become the night once again.
data.name: Night Walker

data.description: The forests have longed for your help, and this last cry shook the world.
data.name: Forest Guardian

data.description: Your very prescence is masked by nature itself.  You are slightly harder to detect.
data.name: Nature's Boon

attacks.attack_text_u: You tear into %s with your blades

data.description: Your torso has an extra set of appendages that have burst out of your back, they are tipped with massive bone blades at the end, and look like they can do some serious damage with the thick acid that they secrete.
data.name: Slashers

data.iv_message: You twitch and pant randomly as your desire to slake your inhuman thirst becomes overwhelming.
data.mutagen_message: Nearby shadows seem to bend towards you for a moment and then reality warps back into place.
data.name: Vampire

data.description: Mutagen cocktail simply labeled 'C.R.I.T R&D.'
data.name: vampire mutagen

data.description: A super-concentrated pitch-black substance with silvery flecks that reminds you of a starry-night sky.  You need a syringe to inject it… if you really want to?
data.name: vampire serum

messages: You have a strange dream about the shadows.
Your dreams give you a peculiar feeling of sinking into the dark.
You have a vivid dream of talking a midnight stroll.
You dream of drinking copious amounts of warm water.
You have a dream of being chased by dogs as something warm drips from your mouth.
Snippets of stalking something in the star-lit night shakes you awake.
You dream of sinking your fangs into more and more enemies.
You have a lucid dream where streams of blood are slowly pooling around your feet.

data.iv_message: A serene feeling of terror grips you as become acutely aware of the flora and fauna beckoning towards you.
data.mutagen_message: Nearby plants seem to bend towards you for a moment and then they shift back into place.
data.name: Wendigo

data.description: Mutagen cocktail simply labeled 'C.R.I.T R&D.'
data.name: wendigo mutagen

data.description: A super-concentrated peat-brown substance with glittering green flecks that reminds you of a a tree.  You need a syringe to inject it… if you really want to?
data.name: wendigo serum

messages: You have a strange dream about the mountain forests.
Your dreams give you a peculiar feeling of sinking into the treelines.
You have a vivid dream of strolling through the woods.
You dream of drinking copious amounts of warm water.
You have a dream of chasing something as a raw hunger sears your mind.
Recollections of stalking a human shakes you awake.
You dream of tearing into more and more enemies.
You have a lucid dream where nature carefully welcomes your body.

data.description: You were training ahead of time to become a Catastrophe Response and Investigation Team officer in the upcoming war.  Your call to arms arrived dead on arrival and already plastered in the all-too vibrant gore of your squadmates.  In the midst of panic, you bugged out lest you join the remnants of your friends.  Now it's up to your wits and short years of training to keep you alive in this Cataclysm.
data.name: C.R.I.T. ROTC Member

data.description: You were a senior NCO for the Catastrophe Response and Investigation Team, relaying orders to your squad was an everyday task.  When the cataclysm struck, your expertise helped save everyone time and time again until it all fell apart.
data.name: C.R.I.T. NCO

data.description: You were part of the infantry; first to hit the ground running, clear a FOB and then come back to relax with your squad.  Those days ended when the cataclysm reared its ugly head.  Your lines were torn through like wet paper when the otherworldly abominations arrived.  Now fleeing for your life, will you have what it takes to survive or is this hellish landscape your resting place?
data.name: C.R.I.T. Grunt

data.description: You were assigned the billet of specializing in creating dead zones and providing support fire.  When the cataclysm struck, your trusty LMG couldn't keep the veritable tide of undead from overtaking your squad.  Now running with all the strength your body can muster, will you have what it takes to survive or is this hellish landscape something you just can't suppress?
data.name: C.R.I.T. Automatic Rifleman

data.description: As a top-ranking CO, you didn't see much in the way of combat other than when you felt like it.  Your charisma and sharp intellect helped you climb up the ranks and provided support to allies in need.  Now that everything went down the drain, will it help you again?
data.name: C.R.I.T. Commanding Officer

data.description: You were scheduled for some survival training in New England when the Cataclysm broke out and your instructor never showed up for the next lesson.  Now stuck in the quarantine zone with your standard issue training equipment, you wish you had a better gun.  Looks like you'll definitely learn a thing or two about survival though!
data.name: C.R.I.T. Recruit

data.description: You were the team's electronics nerd.  A technician trained to operate all the new laser equipment.  You were left behind by you team as repaired a turret. Forced to flee, will you have what it takes to survive or will you join the horde to haunt them for their cardinal sin?
data.name: C.R.I.T. Technician

data.description: You were the team nerd.  A combat medic taught on how to engage strange anomalies.  However, your main focus was  keeping your squadmates in one piece.  For weeks, you slogged through hell and back and ensured that everyone made it.  Caught in between a tide of the undead and the crossfire of apparently rogue government robots, you were left stranded as a distraction.  Forced to flee, will you have what it takes to survive or will you join the horde to haunt them for their cardinal sin?
data.name: C.R.I.T. Combat Medic

data.description: You were scheduled to fix the several of the lab facilities in New England and show other researchers the new weapons you were working on.  When the Cataclysm broke out, it made it so testing was easier to do, but then again nothing seems to making that much sense.  Time to bug-out!
data.name: C.R.I.T. Engineer

data.description: STR 10 recommended.  You were a granted the authority of a U.S Marshal.  As a guard, others joked that you were nothing more than a mall cop with a fancy badge.  Knowingly, you laughed it off as the you donned your equipment and deployed.  While you mainly spent time at base, you honed your skills and received special implants to do your job easier at the low low cost of serving as a "guard" forever.  Time to do your job, mission parameters look like they've expanded quite a bit though.
data.name: C.R.I.T. Enforcer

data.description: STR 10 recommended.  You are fully armored badass granted the full authority of a U.S Marshal.  Sent in as the one man army capable of handling anything.  Dropped into warzones, you singlehandedly laid out entire battalions by yourself.  Time to hang them all.
data.name: C.R.I.T. Lone Wolf

data.description: STR 10 recommended.  You were an elite member of Catastrophe Response and Investigation Team, for a powerful military faction.  A looming spectre which responded to secular threats, with technology decades ahead of other world powers.  Your squad was the first to be deployed into the New England region, ground zero, to contain the impending outbreak and gain information to relay back to command.  Good luck soldier.
data.name: C.R.I.T. Spec Ops

data.description: You were an elite recon of the Catastrophe Response and Investigation Team.  You were hailed as a top survivalist after being stuck for weeks behind enemy lines and having to survive with nothing but some rocks, sticks and plants.  However, after a few too many drinks (20) at the local bar and getting into a fight (knocking them out) with one of your commanding officers during a drunken bout you were stripped of your rank and sent off into the forests with your current gear to run a trial by survival.  After an hour of scouting about in the forest for a good shelter, your radio rang and you were briefed over the fact that the world was suddenly ending.  Of course, no one has time to pick your sorry ass up, so cheers.  Staying away from drinks might be a good idea; at least you got some real tools this time!
data.name: C.R.I.T. Survivalist

data.description: Your base in New England fell to the unholy onslaught of the Cataclysm.  However, as a a top researcher in the R&D department, you had chosen to slowly mutate yourself into something more than human.  Even if the concoction was less than perfect, your old flimsy body feels empowered.  With the new flesh that is now your own, bare your fangs and fight until the next dawn.
data.name: C.R.I.T. Night Walker

data.description: *Sigh* Your life has been a wreck.  Hopping place to place you finally found a job at Catastrophe Response and Investigation Team… as a janitor of sorts.  The pay was good and you got some sneak-peeks on some pretty cool stuff.  After all non-essential personnel were purged you found yourself stuck with nothing but the uniform they gave you and your job's sign-bonus equipment.
data.name: C.R.I.T. Janitor

data.description: Like many others, you had requested to join the Catastrophe Response and Investigation Team organization's admin-offices to escape from bitter memories and past traumas after valiantly protecting your comrades for years.  After you completed the readjustment program, your skills may have rusted considerably since your last deployment to battle, but the drilled muscle memories have not worn away.  As your comrades' screams once again ring in your ears and repressed memories of abhorrent nature resurface, can you find it within yourself to overcome the looming terror which paralyzes you?
data.name: C.R.I.T. Employee

info.description: Allows infestation of non-dirt tiles. Allows fungal breed through fungal stalk.
info.name: Dangerous Fungi

data.info: Enables fungal stalk to be spawned on infested terrain from 'PLANT' attack function.
data.name: MON_FUNGALOID_YOUNG_ALLOWED

data.info: Enables fungal infestation on FLAT tiles (indoors houses/asphalt/etc..).
data.name: FUNGUS_SPREAD_ON_FLAT_TILES_ALLOWED

data.info: Base rate to spawn fungal stalk from 'PLANT' attack function. Chance to spawn: 1 / (MON_FUNGALOID_YOUNG_SPAWN_BASE_RATE + num_creatures_in_bubble / MON_FUNGALOID_YOUNG_SPAWN_BUBBLE_CREATURES_DIVIDER)
data.name: MON_FUNGALOID_YOUNG_SPAWN_BASE_RATE

data.info: Number of creatures in bubble will be divided on that number to calculate chance to spawn fungal stalk from 'PLANT' attack function. Chance to spawn: 1 / (MON_FUNGALOID_YOUNG_SPAWN_BASE_RATE + num_creatures_in_bubble / MON_FUNGALOID_YOUNG_SPAWN_BUBBLE_CREATURES_DIVIDER)
data.name: MON_FUNGALOID_YOUNG_SPAWN_BUBBLE_CREATURES_DIVIDER

data.info: Chance (0 to 1) to trigger spore creation on fungal_effects::fungalize method (for character; infested furniture/terrain bash; FUNGUS death function, FUNGUS_ADVANCED attack function) (note: change for FUNGUS_ADVANCED attack function will be affected by random radius change (1~2) and will proportionally decrease as FUNGUS_ADVANCED_CREATURES_THRESHOLD is passed)
data.name: FUNGUS_SPORE_CHANCE

data.info: If number of creatures nearby will pass this threshold - chance to spawn spore by FUNGUS_ADVANCED attack will be proportionally decreased
data.name: FUNGUS_ADVANCED_CREATURES_THRESHOLD

data.info: Global value that affects ability of fungal_effects::fungalize to spawn spores. If there is more creature nearby than specified value - spore will not spawn
data.name: FUNGUS_SPORE_CREATURES_THRESHOLD

info.description: A testbed/WIP mod to showcase regional_map_settings JSON changes.
info.name: Desert Region

data.description: A menacing arachnid with a second set of pedipalps, clacking rabidly as an engorged stinger looms over its body.

data.//1: Capping these at 1000 L and kg to prevent bodies and meat disappearing
data.//2: Baby elephants don't actually exist (yet), but Spring is the wet season (their mating season) so baby_flags is defined so that they get angry then
data.description: The African Bush Elephant is a massive terrestrial animal with two long tusks protruding from its mouth.  Elephantry is on the rise since the Cataclysm.

petfood.feed: The %s seems to like you! It lets you pat its head and seems friendly.

data.description: Grotesque and deformed kangaroo body that has black goo seeping from its orifices.  The most unnerving is its forearms have reformed into a raptorial shape, like that of a praying mantis.
A horrifying image with tendrils seeping out of many oozing wounds, this elephant's form appears more eldritch than animal.
A calloused and misshapened form that seems to have adapted to the harsh, dry environment.
A reptile with aposematic coloration of black and orange that is renowned for an extremely painful venom when it bites.

data.//: Missing feature to produce whistling noise when walked on.
data.description: A formation of sand containing special minerals to produce whistling noises when stepped on and from the wind.
data.name: singing sand

data.//: Missing feature to allow survivors to climb to increase overmap vision range.
data.description: An assortment of various large, sharp rocks that jut out from the surrounding terrain.  Could be as a surface for a number of things or climbed on if worn with proper footwear.
data.name: outcrop

data.description: A huge sand dune that is continually reshaped by the wind.  Climbing it will be troublesome.
data.name: dune

data.description: Not that kind of pavement!  Naturally occurring pockets of densely packed rock fragments that is easier to traverse than sand, with the right footwear.
data.name: desert pavement

data.description: Cracked and dry soil that doesn't look like it will be capable of supporting anything in your lifetime.
data.name: scorched earth

data.description: A tall cactus.
data.name: cactus

info.description: Strength checks and lifting qualities are no longer required for installing or removing vehicle parts.
info.name: No Lifting Reqs

info.description: 9x18mm and similar ammo types, but now easily contained in a mod so that people can turn them off if they consider them bloat.  Should be a dependency of any mod that uses the ammo types.
info.name: Exotic ammo types

data.description: .270 Winchester ammunition with 130gr soft point bullets.  The .270 round was not initially successful, but over a few decades it became one of the most popular rifle cartridges for hunting and silhouette shooting.  It is a powerful round capable of taking down large targets with ease.

data.//: Base damage of 26, balance increase of two-nineths.
data.description: .38 Super ammunition with 147gr FMJ bullets.  The .38 Super round was developed from .38 ACP in the 1920s, designed to penetrate the body armor of the era.

data.//: Hollowpoint damage increase of 25%.
data.description: .38 Super ammunition with 90gr JHP bullets.  Designed in 1929, the .38 super cartridge was designed to penetrate body armor and vehicles.

.454 Casull ammunition with 300gr jacketed soft point bullets.  The .454 Casull round is derived from .45 Long Colt with a stronger, lengthened case.  It is an exceptionally powerful cartridge with higher stopping power than many rifle rounds, although it suffers from extreme recoil making it unsuitable for most purposes.

data.//: Base damage of 20, balance increase of one third.
data.description: 4.6x30mm ammunition with 31gr copper plated steel bullets.  The 4.6x30mm round was developed by H&K to compete with FN Herstal's 5.7x28mm cartridge.  It has low recoil and excellent armor penetration.

4.6x30mm ammunition with 31gr copper plated lead bullets.  The 4.6x30mm round was developed by H&K to compete with FN Herstal's 5.7x28mm cartridge.  It has low recoil, but no usual armor penetration due to using simple lead bullets rather than proper factory-made penetrator projectiles.
A plated, hard cast flat nosed variant of the .460 Rowland round.  This increases penetration slightly at the cost of reduced damage from expansion.

.460 Rowland ammunition with 185gr JHP bullets.  The .460 Rowland round is designed to emulate hard-hitting .44 magnum in common autoloading pistols.  Like the .44, .460 Rowland has exceptional power - and recoil.  It is almost dimensionally identical to .45ACP, save for having a longer case and deeper seated bullets to prevent ammunition mix-ups.

.500 S&W Magnum ammunition with 500gr bullets.  The .500 S&W Magnum round is a colossally powerful handgun cartridge capable of killing almost any target with one hit.  It has extremely high damage and recoil to match.

data.//: Mainlined from Icecoon's Weapons Pack.
data.description: 5.45x39mm 7N10 ammunition with 56gr FMJ bullets.  The 5.45x39mm round was introduced along with the AK-74 in 1974.  It has superior wounding potential to the older 7.62x39mm round and quickly replaced it in Soviet military use.

data.//: Balanced as AP, 7/8 damage, arpen 6/7 that value.
data.description: Armor piercing 5.45x39mm ammunition with 57gr bullets containing a steel penetrator.  Developed in 1998 by the Russian military.

data.//: Base damage of 20, balance increase of one third.  Armor penetration balanced on the assumption that this is the AP variant of a hypothetical FMJ round.
data.description: 5.7x28mm ammunition with 31gr AP FMJ bullets.  The 5.7x28mm cartridge was designed by FN Herstal to replace the 9x19mm round in NATO use.  Although the project to replace 9x19mm Parabellum was effectively canceled the 5.7x28mm round has seen action in many conflicts and has proven to be reliable.  It has very low recoil and its armor penetration is a defining feature.

5.7x28mm ammunition with 31gr AP FMJ bullets.  The 5.7x28mm cartridge was designed by FN Herstal to replace the 9x19mm round in NATO use.  Although the project to replace 9x19mm Parabellum was effectively canceled the 5.7x28mm round has seen action in many conflicts and has proven to be reliable.  It has very low recoil but no usual armor penetration due to using simple lead bullets rather than proper factory-made penetrator projectiles.

data.//: Base damage of 110, balance reduction to roughly 80%.
data.description: The .700 Nitro Express is a very powerful rifle round designed for long-range use.  Its stupendous accuracy and armor piercing capabilities make it one of the most deadly rounds available, offset only by its drastic recoil and noise.
The .700 Nitro Express is a very powerful rifle round designed for long-range use.  The "Nitro" in the name isn't very applicable to this blackpowder load, but it still has plenty of Express.

data.//: Base damage of 24, balance increase of two-nineths.
data.description: A commercial version of the 7.62x25mm cartridge created for the armed forces of Soviet Russia.  It was derived from the 7.63x25mm cartridge used by the C96 pistol.

data.//: Hollowpoint bonus of 25%, retains armor penetration due to being a +P load.
data.description: A high-pressure commercial version of the 7.62x25mm cartridge, loaded with an 85 gr. FMJ bullet.  It is more powerful than the original.

data.//: Stopgap price of $1 per.  Anti-China prejudice in the pre-Cataclysm US may have made this ammo tougher to come by.
data.//2: AP variant, 7/8 damage relative to FMJ variant, armor penetration matches damage.
data.description: A subsonic cartridge derived from the 7.62x25mm, designed for silenced firearms.  It offers good armor penetration at the cost of slightly less damage.

data.//: Base damage of 16, balance increase of one-third.
data.description: 9x18mm Makarov ammunition with 93gr steel core FMJ bullets.  The 9x18mm round was very common in the Eastern Bloc during the 20th century and remained in Russian military service into the 21st century.
9x18mm Makarov SP-7 ammunition.  The 9x18mm round was very common in the Eastern Bloc during the 20th century and remained in Russian military service into the 21st century.

data.//: AP variant, 7/8 damage relative to FMJ variant, armor penetration matches damage.
data.description: 9x18mm Makarov RG028 ammunition.  The RG028 round uses bullets with a hardened steel core to improve armor penetration.
An empty casing from a 9x18mm round.
An empty casing from a .38 Super round.
An empty casing from a .700 NX round.  These are rare, so you might want to hold onto these.
An empty casing from a .460 Rowland round.  It looks deceptively like a .45ACP casing.
An empty casing from a .454 Casull round.
An empty casing from a .500 S&W Magnum round.
An empty casing from a 5.7x28mm round.
An empty casing from a 4.6x30mm round.
An empty casing from a 5.45x39mm round.
An empty casing from a .270 Winchester round.
An empty casing from a 7.62x25mm round.

This not-so-little bundle of joy contains Leadworks, LLC's premier offerings: the Enforcer autorevolver (with holster) and Long Ranger rifle.  Maintenance kit, batteries for same, and a complimentary copy of the Gun Owner's Handbook are included.  The perfect investment for a new family starting out!  Disassemble to unpack and enjoy.

A beautiful wooden stock and hardware built to support common Mosin-Nagant actions.  With gunsmithing tools, some skill, and a few hours, you could take a 'tacti-cool' rifle and convert it to the classic Russian design.

A synthetic stock and hardware designed to accommodate common Mosin-Nagant actions.  With gunsmithing tools, some skill, and a few hours, you could give the classic Russian design modern furniture and customization capacity.

All the parts necessary to convert an L523 to the Carbine configuration, Leadworks LLC's choice for close-quarters work.  In order to reconfigure an already-configured L523, you'll need to disassemble it to the base platform, then assemble with the parts you prefer.

All the parts necessary to convert an L523 to a Main Battle Rifle, a balanced weapon for all situations.  In order to reconfigure an already-configured L523, you'll need to disassemble it to the base platform, then assemble with the parts you prefer.

All the parts necessary to convert an L523 to a Designated Marksman Rifle, suitable for long-ranged work.  In order to reconfigure an already-configured L523, you'll need to disassemble it to the base platform, then assemble with the parts you prefer.

All the parts necessary to convert an L523 to a light machine gun.  In order to reconfigure an already-configured L523, you'll need to disassemble it to the base platform, then assemble with the parts you prefer.

A classic bolt action rifle chambered for .270 Winchester, very popular among hunters.  This is a CDL SF model with a forged, fluted, 416 stainless steel barrel threaded into the receiver and a recessed bolt face.  It has checkered walnut furniture and a recoil pad to reduce perceived recoil.
A double-barrel semi-automatic pistol of Italian origin, firing two bullets per shot, a derivative of the M1911 pistol.
The M1911A1 is an extremely popular pistol known for its reliability.  This one is chambered for .38 Super.
The Taurus Raging Bull is a 5-shot revolver chambered in .454 Casull.  It has impressive stopping power.
The Taurus Raging Judge Magnum is a 6-shot revolver chambered in .454 Casull.  It can fire .410 shotshells and .45 Colt cartridges as well.
Designed as a personal defense weapon, the MP7 fires the high-powered 4.6x30mm round while being lightweight, compact in size, and practically recoil free.

This 1911 has been customized to chamber powerful .460 Rowland ammunition.  It now sports a large, integral compensator to aid in taming recoil and reducing slide velocity.  Notably, normal .45ACP can also be fired through the weapon.
Built in the image of the venerable Winchester Model 1886, Big Horn Armory's Model 89 rifle packs all the power of the .500 S&W Magnum cartridge in to a package less likely to break one's wrist.
The 5-shot Smith and Wesson 500 revolver fires the comparably-named .500 S&W Magnum.  It's an impressive weapon.

data.//: AKs likewise aren't commercially traded in the US, plus this is newer.
data.description: The successor to the well-known AK-47 rifle.  It combines the reliability of the AK series with the high-velocity, lightweight 5.45x39mm cartridge.

Intended to replace the AK-74, this rifle uses a sophisticated mechanism to delay felt recoil, along with a very fast two-round burst mode.  While its increased complexity prevented it from being adopted by the Russian military, it has seen service among their special forces.
Designed to work with FN's proprietary 5.7x28mm round, the Five-Seven is a lightweight pistol with a very high capacity, best used against armored opponents.
The first in a new genre of guns, termed "personal defense weapons."  FN designed the P90 to use their proprietary 5.7x28mm ammunition.  It is made for firing bursts manageably.
A ornately engraved, custom-made double rifle specially designed for the hunting of huge game.  You could obviously kill everything with this, EVERYTHING.  If you ever find enough ammo of course.
The Soviet-made PPSh-41 is a mass-produced selective-fire submachine gun.  It has a relatively high rate of fire.

data.//: And it's therefore expensive.
data.description: The Tokarev TT-33 is an antique Russian semiautomatic pistol, famous for its durability, accuracy, and uncomfortable grip angle.  It was chambered for the 7.62x25mm due to the popularity of the C96 pistol among Russian revolutionaries.

data.//: 10 and 12-round magazines exist, but were made for the newer PMM, and are not compatible with the old PM.
data.description: The Pistolet Makarova was developed by the Soviet Union to replace the WWII-era TT-33 pistol.  It uses the 9x18mm cartridge, which remains in use among various former Soviet countries.

data.//: Total unloaded weight of gun 1451.496 grams, rounded to 1451.  Current weight of folding stock 200 grams.
data.description: A version of the Skorpion submachine gun chambered in 9x18mm Makarov, with a slightly longer barrel than the original design.

data.description: This unusual Rivtech carbine features five rotating barrels and a large capacity helical magazine.  Durable and easy to handle, it was designed to overcome the deficiencies of its low power .22LR cartridge by rapidly delivering a large number of projectiles.

data.//: Most real-life autorevolvers use 6-7.5 inch barrel, but this one's mentioned to be particularly massive.
data.description: The logical extension of Leadworks' autorevolver series, this massive 'sidearm' fires 5.56 NATO to share ammo with a soldier's rifle.  The muzzle flash and report of this 5-shot revolver are incredible, producing ballistics similar to .357 Magnum.  Similar to other Leadworks products it doesn't accept third-party modifications.

data.//: Current milspec, so Expensive.
data.description: Leadworks LLC's bullpup L523 platform utilizes the ubiquitous 5.56 round in multiple applications, and is designed to part-swap quickly, safely, and effectively from one to the next.  The base mechanism could--theoretically--be used on its own, but such use is inadvisable and will void your warranty.  There's no real place to incorporate aftermarket modifications on this barren proto-weapon.

Leadworks LLC's bullpup L523 platform utilizes the ubiquitous 5.56 round in multiple applications, and is designed to part-swap quickly, safely, and effectively from one to the next.  The carbine model incorporates Leadworks' proprietary gyro-stabilization technology and includes a red-dot sight and an integral laser sight.  Additionally it comes with underslung shotgun for close range encounters.  Like the rest of the family, doesn't accept third-party modifications.

Leadworks LLC's bullpup L523 platform utilizes the ubiquitous 5.56 round in multiple applications, and is designed to part-swap quickly, safely, and effectively from one to the next.  The DMR incorporates a specially-designed extended barrel with integral bipod and foregrip, along with an integral 8x optic, and trades selective-fire for the Leadworks gyro-stabilization system.  Like the rest of the family, though, it doesn't accept third-party modifications.

relative.//: Volume, range, and bullpup all point to a very large barrel on this one.

data.description: Leadworks LLC's bullpup L523 platform utilizes the ubiquitous 5.56 round in multiple applications, and is designed to part-swap quickly, safely, and effectively from one to the next.  The rather ambitious light machine gun variant incorporates ACOG scope, a heavy-duty barrel, foregrip, bipod and as well as the Leadworks gyro system.  Unlike the other configurations, the L523 LMG can be loaded with ammo belts.

Leadworks LLC's bullpup L523 platform utilizes the ubiquitous 5.56 round in multiple applications, and is designed to part-swap quickly, safely, and effectively from one to the next.  The battle rifle's (proprietary) combination optic & red-dot sights provide quick and accurate targeting, with Leadworks gyro-stabilization system and foregrip available for greater control.  Additionally it comes with grenade launcher module for dealing with hard targets.  Like the rest of the family, though, it doesn't accept third-party modifications.

Developed as a testament to the L39 family of pistols' strength, this hefty pistol chambers powerful .460 Rowland.  In addition to the heavy slide and L39 standard sighting package, this pistol sports a large compensator.  Notably, normal .45ACP can also be fired through the weapon.  Similar to other Leadworks products it doesn't accept third-party modifications.

After the success of their 9x19mm machine pistol, Leadworks LLC developed the L39B-45 from their .460 Rowland semi-autos, intending them to be used with .45ACP.  Because it shares most of its parts with the L39-460, this variant retains the ability to fire fearsome .460 Rowland, though users are strongly advised not to do so during automatic fire.  Comes with built in red dot, recoil compensator and laser sights.  Similar to other Leadworks products it doesn't accept third-party modifications.

data.//: Real-life Autorevolvers tended to vary between 6-7.5 inch barrels, so we'll err on the side of a +1 here.
data.description: An "autorevolver", this Leadworks LLC six-shooter combines the ease-of-action of a semi-auto with the ease-of-reloading and style of a revolver.  In addition to ubiquitous .45 ACP, the Enforcer also fires powerful .460 Rowland, offering .44 Magnum performance in a smaller package.  It achieved overnight stardom when it appeared in "Treasure Trail", a popular space-Western series.  Comes with built in holographic and laser sights.  Similar to other Leadworks products it doesn't accept third-party modifications.

data.//: If it's based off the Winchester 94, it's likely got a 20 inch barrel. For sanity's sake though, let's cap it at +4.
data.description: The Leadworks Long Ranger was designed to give Enforcer users (or anyone using .460/.45 caliber cartridges) a little extra reach while using the same ammunition.  Loaded with powerful .460 Rowland ammunition, this rifle offers a competitive replacement to the AR-15 in politically restrictive climates, but was met with disgust from lever action purists because of its synthetic furniture and built in holographic and laser sights.  Similar to other Leadworks products it doesn't accept third-party modifications.

After the Enforcer's success, Leadworks LLC reworked their autorevolver design to cater to the 9x19mm market.  The Lookout takes advantage of the smaller 9x19mm to hold a seventh round, without sacrificing the ease of handling or reloading the Enforcer made legendary.  Comes with built in holographic and laser sights.  Similar to other Leadworks products it doesn't accept third-party modifications.

data.//: The M93R can go through up to 110 rounds in six seconds, FWIW.
data.description: A selective-firing 9x19mm handgun introduced by Leadworks LLC's, L39B provides accurate single-shot placement as well as controllable short-range firepower with its three-round burst mode.  Users praised its accuracy and fearsome appearance.  Comes with built in red dot and laser sights.  Similar to other Leadworks products it doesn't accept third-party modifications.

data.//: L39s are based off the Robocop Auto-9, in turn a dolled-up Beretta 93R.
data.description: A semiautomatic 9x19mm handgun introduced by Leadworks LLC's, L39 provides accurate single-shot placement in a very robust frame with built in red dot and laser sights.  Several urban legends describe using it in ways that would have destroyed a lesser weapon.  Similar to other Leadworks products it doesn't accept third-party modifications.

info.description: In the wake of the Cataclysm sweets and snacks are coming to life.  You could be one of them and walk through the Cataclysm as a human shaped piece of sugar with your pet necco wafer, or you could just hunt them for some sweet treats.
info.name: My Sweet Cataclysm: BN Edition

data.description: A small graham cracker kid, running around on its graham cracker legs.
A fully grown graham cracker, running around on its graham cracker legs.
A small cookie, scuriying around in search of crumbs.
A giant piece of gum, stretched in the shape of a spider.  It stands very still in its gum web.
A giant piece of gum, stretched in the shape of a spider.  It moves quickly and aggressively; as if under the effect of some stimulant.
A cute bear cub made of sugary gelatin.  You wonder if it will grow big one day.
A big bear made of fruit flavored gelatin, its smooth round shape and its fruity smell make it less scary than its fleshy counterpart.
A small humanoid made of marshmallow.  It bumbles around on its stubby cushioned legs.  How cute!
A marshmallow humanoid with a smile drawn on its face.  It bumbles around, hollow eyes scanning its surroundings, seemingly looking for something.
A muscular body made of marshmallow, proudly striding towards an unknown goal.  Yummy!
A gigantic marshmallow humanoid softly stomping around, frozen smile and big empty eyes carefully scanning its surroundings.
A small humanoid made of marsmallow.  It is armored in chocolate coated graham crackers and bumbles around on its stubby, cushioned legs.  How cute!
A marshmallow humanoid in full graham cracker knight armor.  It bumbles around, hollow eyes scanning its surrounding seemingly looking for something.
Standing tall in its shining armor of chocolate coated graham crackers, this marshmallow is proudly striding towards an unknown goal.
A gigantic humanoid armored with thick plates of chocolate coated graham crackers.  A frozen smile is half visible under its heavy helmet, as it carefully scans its surroundings.
A giant necco wafer, happily jaunting around.
An ointment made of caramel.  You could use it to heal your wounds, if you were made of sugar.
data.name: caramel ointment

data.description:
A flyer for some kind of candy.  It shows a picture of a gleaming human made of smooth candy looking at you in terror.  "SugarKin the first life-size human candy!  Are you a real monster?  Will you be able to devour it?"
 On the back of the flyer you can see some hastily scribbled words:
 "Hello, my child, welcome to this world.  A world where you'll be able to thrive if you follow a few rules:
1) Never ever get into contact with water, it would melt you!
2) Avoid humans with clear eyes they are very dangerous!  (You can ignore the ones with black eyes they are harmless to you.)
3) Learn how to make caramel ointment, it's the only way to fix your body if you get hurt.
 There are many more things I'd like to tell you but I must leave before it's too late.  I've made you a friend to keep you company, be kind to it.
 I love you,
 - F.  ".
The corpse of a necco, now it really looks like a giant necco wafer.  Surely a bite wouldn't hurt, right?
data.name: necco corpse

data.description: A handful of squishy, fluffy, puffy, delicious marshmallows.  They feel oddly warm…
A pair of graham crackers with some chocolate and a marshmallow between them.  Warms radiates from it, as if it was… alive?
A handful of colorful fruit-flavored chewy candy.  They're warm to the touch and you can feel a slow beat coming from them.  How odd…
Dry and sugary, these crackers will leave you thirsty, but go good with some chocolate and marshmallows.  They shiver under your touch.  Weird!
data.name: graham cracker

data.description: Sweet and delicious cookies, just like grandma used to bake.  They shiver under your touch.  Weird!
data.name: cookie

data.description: Sugary chewing gum wrapped in bright pink packaging, with a question mark on the label.  It's a mystery what flavor it is, but the label claims there's 20 unique flavors.  It's oddly warm to the touch…
Chewing gum with added caffeine.  Sugary and bad for your teeth, but it's a nice pick-me-up.  It's oddly warm to the touch…

data.apply_message: You're covered in gum!
desc: You have some gum on you.
You are covered in gum!
You're trapped in gum!
miss_messages: The gum webs constrict your movement.
name: Gummed

bash.msg_success: You brush the gum web aside.

data.iv_message: You inject some more fizz into your syrup.
data.mutagen_message: How sweet!
data.name: Sugar Kin

data.description: You are literally made of sugar, giving you 10 cut armor, 5 bash armor, and making you immune to bleeding, radiation, and thirst, but it also prevents you from installing CBMs, prevents you from eating anything but sugar, and you can only heal using caramel ointment.
You're a human shaped candy brought to life under unknown circumstances.  You have your whole life ahead of you and it's gonna be sweet!

You open your eyes as if for the first time, looking at yourself you can see your skin gleam in the light.  One word come to your mind "sugar" you don't remember where you learned it, in fact you don't remember anything.  You don't know why but you feel hopeful, everything feels new and you're going to make the best of it!
data.name: The Sweet Life

text.text: A flyer for some kind of candy.  It shows a picture of a gleaming human made of smooth candy looking at you in terror.  "SugarKin the first life-size human candy!  Are you a real monster?  Will you be able to devour it all?"

info.description: Stockfish-level AI pathfinding: allow complete and unlimited pathfinding for AI regardless of intended enemy intelligence. Additionally, enables pathing around mobs
info.name: Pathfinding: Stockfish AI

data.//: Temporary option, should be removed once intelligence presets are implemented
data.info: If true, default is applied even if the value IS specified. This makes all monsters pathfind the same.
data.name: PATHFINDING_DEFAULT_IS_OVERRIDE

data.info: If `h_coeff` is unspecified, it will be this: how directed the pathfinding is. A value of 1.0 makes pathfinding equivalent to A*, 0.0 is raw Dijikstra; this adjusts precision, high values will converge quicker, but produce a possibly less than shortest path.
data.name: PATHFINDING_H_COEFF_DEFAULT

data.info: If `alpha` is unspecified, it will be this: alpha coefficient is a coefficient that makes pathfinding sometimes take slightly less than most optimal tiles. 1.0 only allows taking the best tiles, 0 will pick any _better_ tile that current one, not necessarily the best one. Use it to make pathfinding more 'flowy' and to decongest routes
data.name: PATHFINDING_ALPHA_DEFAULT

data.info: If `search_radius_coeff` is unspecified, the value will be this: limits pathfinding search domain, will include tiles within the circle centered at the midpoint between start position and end position of diameter equal to the distance between said points multiplied by this coefficient. Setting this less that 1 will make pathfinding always fail. Negative values will disable search radius limit. Be aware, this forces pathfinding to be redone on each call, which is expensive.
data.name: PATHFINDING_SEARCH_RADIUS_COEFF_DEFAULT

data.info: If `search_cone_angle` is unspecified, the value will be this: limits pathfinding search domain, will include tiles within a cone of this angle starting from start position and going towards end position. A value of 180 and more disables search cone. Be aware, this forces pathfinding to be redone on each call, which is expensive.
data.name: PATHFINDING_SEARCH_CONE_ANGLE_DEFAULT

data.info: Multiply specified `max_dist` and `max_length` by this value. If set to negative, makes both infinite, allowing any distance pathfinding.
data.name: PATHFINDING_RANGE_MULT

data.info: Use specified `max_dist` instead of distance between the start position and the end position when limiting search domain based on unbiased f-values: this replicates `max_length` constraint used by legacy pathfinding. This makes maximum allowed produced path complexity depend on how close the target position is to the start position.
data.name: PATHFINDING_MAX_F_LIMIT_BASED_ON_MAX_DIST

data.info: If max_f_coeff is unspecified, the value will be this: limits pathfinding search domain based on unbiased f-values: this will make pathfinding not succeed when the f-value is more than this value multiplied by either the distance between the start position and the end position or by `max_dist` is the option is enabled. 2.5 is equivalent to legacy pathfinding, negative values disable the limit.
data.name: PATHFINDING_MAX_F_COEFF_DEFAULT

data.info: If mob avoidance is unspecified, the value will be this. This makes pathfinding route around living mobs with higher values increasing the route-around range. Negative values will make pathfinding treat mobs as impassable walls.
data.name: PATHFINDING_MOB_PRESENCE_PENALTY_DEFAULT

data.description: A transgender pride flag.  The blue represents transmascs, the pink represents transfemmes, and the white represents non-binary individuals.
The original pride flag of the entire LGBTQ+ community.  The rainbow of colors represents the rainbow of individuals throughout the community.

An aromantic pride flag.  The greens represent aromanticism and the aro-spectrum as a whole, the white represents the importance of non-romantic relationships, and the black and grey represent the sexuality spectrum that ranges from aro-ace to aro-allosexual.
An asexual pride flag.  The black represents total asexuality, the grey represents demisexuals and grey-asexuals, the white represents allies, and the purple represents community.
A bisexual pride flag.  The pink represents attraction to the same sex, the blue represents attraction to the opposite sex, and the purple overlap represents the combination of both.
An intersex pride flag.  The center circle symbolizes wholeness and completeness.

A lesbian pride flag.  The dark orange represents gender non-conformity, the orange represents independence, the white represents unique relationships to womanhood, pink represents serenity and peace, and the dark rose represents feminimity.

A non-binary pride flag.  Yellow represents those who identify outside of the gender binary, white represents those with multiple genders, purple for those with a mix of both male and female, and black for agender individuals.
A pansexual pride flag.  Pink represents being attracted to women, blue being attracted to men, and yellow being attracted to everyone else.

A gay pride flag for men.  The greens represent healing and joy; the white stripe includes people who are transgender, nonbinary, intersex, or otherwise likely to be traditionally left out; the blues represent pure love, strength, and diversity.

A genderfluid pride flag.  The pink represents feminimity, the white represents lack of gender, the purple represents androgyny, the black represents all other genders, and the blue represents masculinity.

The autistic pride flag.  Gold middle band refers to the elemental symbol of gold (AU) which is used in the autistic community.  The gradient refers to the Pride Movement.  Red originated as a protest to the `light it up blue` campaign, which was denounced by the autistic community.  The infinity sign is a symbol long used in the wider autistic and wider neurodiverse community.

data.description: An old metal flagpole, now flying the infinity symbol upon an autism pride flag.  Gold middle band refers to the elemental symbol of gold (AU) which is used in the autistic community.  The gradient refers to the Pride Movement.  Red originated as a protest to the `light it up blue` campaign, which was denounced by the autistic community.  The infinity sign is a symbol long used in the wider autistic and wider neurodiverse community.
data.name: flagpole with Autism pride flag

data.description: This makeshift flagpole is currently flying the infinity symbol upon an autism pride flag.  Gold middle band refers to the elemental symbol of gold (AU) which is used in the autistic community.  The gradient refers to the Pride Movement.  Red originated as a protest to the `light it up blue` campaign, which was denounced by the autistic community.  The infinity sign is a symbol long used in the wider autistic and wider neurodiverse community.
data.name: wooden flagpole with Autism pride flag

A non-binary pride flag, fluttering in the breeze atop a vehicle.  Yellow represents those who identify outside of the gender binary, white represents those with multiple genders, purple for those with a mix of both male and female, and black for agender individuals.

A genderfluid pride flag, fluttering in the breeze atop a vehicle.  The pink represents feminimity, the white represents lack of gender, the purple represents androgyny, the black represents all other genders, and the blue represents masculinity.
